but does not take instruction himself.

He preaches at length

but does not accept any preaching for himself.

He is tall on speech

but short on action.

He aspires for things that will perish

and ignores things that will last for good.

He regards profit as loss and loss as profit.

He fears death but does nothing in its anticipation.

He regards the sins of others as big

but considers the same things for himself as small.

If he does something in obedience to Allah, he considers it much,

but if others do the same, he considers it small.

He, therefore, rebukes others but flatters himself.

Entertainment in the company of the wealthy

is dearer to him than remembrance (of Allah) with the poor;

he passes verdicts against others for his own interests

and does not do so against himself for others’ interests.

He guides others but misguides himself.

He is obeyed by others but he himself disobeys (Allah).

He seeks the fulfillment of obligations (to himself)

but does not fulfil his own obligations (towards others).

He fears the people (and deeds) for others besides his Lord (Allah)

and does not fear his Lord in dealing with the public.

Sayyid ar-Radi says the following:

if this book had contained nothing save this short statement,

it will have sufficed as a successful piece of preaching,

a specimen of high philosophy,

an objective of wisdom for the onlooker

and a source of instruction for the meditative onlooker.

title.en: Sayings 151 The necessity of futurism

content.ar:
ذکر المستقبل

قال علیه السلام : لکل امرى عاقبه حلوه او مره.
content.en:
The necessity of futurism

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Every human being has to meet the end sweet or sour

title.en: Sayings 152 paying attention to morality

content.ar:
ضروره ذکر فنا الدنیا

قال علیه السلام : لکل مقبل ادبار، وما ادبر کان لم یکن.
content.en:
paying attention to morality

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Every comer has to return, and after returning it is as though he never existed

title.en: Sayings 153 Patience and victory

content.ar:
الصبر و الظفر

قال علیه السلام : لا یعدم الصبور الظفر وان طال به الزمان.
content.en:
Patience and victory

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: The one who endures does not miss success although it may take a long time

title.en: Sayings 154 The importance of intentions

content.ar:
الرضی بفعل قوم

قال علیه السلام : الراضی بفعل قوم کالداخل فیه معهم، وعلى کل داخل فی باطل اثمان: اثم العمل به، واثم الرضى به.
content.en:
The importance of intentions

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Whoever agrees with the action of a group of persons is as though he joins them in that action. And everyone who joins in wrong commits two sins: one sin for committing the wrong and the other for agreeing with it

title.en: Sayings 155 The necessity of adhering to the promises

content.ar:
ضروره العمل بالعهود

قال علیه السلام : اعتصموا بالذمم فی اوتادها.
content.en:
The necessity of adhering to the promises

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Adhere to your covenants and entrust their fulfillment to steadfast persons

title.en: Sayings 156 The necessity of  theology and obedience from Him

content.ar:
معرفه الله و طاعته

قال علیه السلام : علیکم بطاعه من لا تعذرون بجهالته
content.en:
The necessity of theology and obedience from Him

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: On you lies (the obligation of) obedience to the person about whom you cannot plead the excuse of ignorance.

title.en: Sayings 157 Availability of guidance ways

content.ar:
الانسان و الهدایه

قال علیه السلام : قد بصرتم ان ابصرتم وقد هدیتم ان اهتدیتم، واسمعتم ان استمعتم.
content.en:
Availability of guidance ways

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Your minds are opened when you see and you are guided when you receive guidance, and you are made to hear when you do hear

title.en: Sayings 158 How to deal with bad friends
title.ar: الحکمة 158 کیفیة التعامل مع اصدقاء السّوء

content.ar:
کیفیه التعامل مع اصدقا السو

قال علیه السلام : عاتب اخاک بالاحسان الیه، واردد شره بالانعام علیه.
content.en:
How to deal with bad friends

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Remonstrate with your brother by being kind to him and respond to his evil by being generous to him.

title.en: Sayings 159 Avoidance of accusation positions

content.ar:
التحذیر من مواضع التهم

قال علیه السلام : من وضع نفسه مواضع التهمه فلا یلومن من اسا به الظن.
content.en:
Avoidance of accusation positions

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: If one places himself where he may be charged, let him not blame those who may think ill of him.

title.en: Sayings 160 Power and violence

content.ar:
القدره و العدوان

قال علیه السلام : من ملک استاث .
content.en:
Power and violence

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: One who possesses prefers himself over others.

title.en: Sayings 161 The value of consultation and avoid egocentrism

content.ar:
فضل المشوره

قال علیه السلام : ومن استبد برایه هلک، ومن شاور الرجال شارکها فی عقولها.
content.en:
The value of consultation and avoid egocentrism

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: One who follows only his view perishes, and one who consults with others shares their wisdom

title.en: Sayings 162 The necessity of secrecy

content.ar:
ضروره حفظ السر

قال علیه السلام : ومن کتم سره. کانت الخیره بیده
content.en:
The necessity of secrecy

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: One who hides his secret holds his own option.

title.en: Sayings 163 Poverty and destruction

content.ar:
الفقر و الموت

قال علیه السلام : الفقر الموت الاکبر
content.en:
Poverty and destruction

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Poverty is the greatest death.

title.en: Sayings 164 Methods of dealing with offenders

content.ar:
اسلوب مواجهه العدو

قال علیه السلام : من قضى حق من لا یقضی حقه فقد عبده.
content.en:
Methods of dealing with offenders

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: If one is fair to another who is not fair to him, it is as though he worships him.

title.en: Sayings 165 Avoiding from disobedience of God

content.ar:
التحذیر من معصیه الله

قال علیه السلام : لا طاعه لمخلوق فی معصیه الخالق.
content.en:
Avoiding from disobedience of God

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: No creature should be obeyed so long as such obedience means disobedience of the Creator.

title.en: Sayings 166 Avoiding from infringement of others' rights

content.ar:
التحذیر من العدوان

قال علیه السلام : لا یعاب المر بتاخیر حقه انما یعاب من اخذ ما لیسله.
content.en:
Avoiding from infringement of others' rights

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: No blame should be placed on one in delaying taking what belongs to him; blame should be on one who takes what does not belong to him.

title.en: Sayings 167 Egotism and deprivations

content.ar:
خطر الاعجاب

قال علیه السلام : الاعجاب یمنع من الازدیاد
content.en:
Egotism and deprivations

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Conceit stops one from seeking perfection

title.en: Sayings 168 Paying attention to mortality of the world

content.ar:
ضروره ذکر فنا الدنیا

قال علیه السلام : الامر قریب والاصطحاب قلیل.
content.en:
Paying attention to mortality of the world

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: The undertaking is near but company is not.

title.en: Sayings 169 Futurism

content.ar:
ذکر المستقبل

قال علیه السلام : قد اضا الصبح لذی عینین.
content.en:
Futurism

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Morning surely shines for those who have vision.

title.en: Sayings 170 The necessity of abandoning a sin

content.ar:
ضروره ترک المعصیه

قال علیه السلام : ترک الذنب اهون من طلب التوبه.
content.en:
The necessity of abandoning a sin

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Abandoning a sin is easier than seeking help

title.en: Sayings 171 The ominous consequence of usury

content.ar:
التحذیر من اکله الحرام

قال علیه السلام : کم من اکله منعت اکلات!
content.en:
The ominous consequence of usury

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: How often one meal blocks many!

title.en: Sayings 172 Ignorance and enmities

content.ar:
الجهل و العداوه

قال علیه السلام : الناس اعدا ما جهلوا.
content.en:
Ignorance and enmities

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: People are enemies of what they do not know

title.en: Sayings 173 The value of consultations

content.ar:
فضل المشوره

قال علیه السلام : من استقبل وجوه الارا عرف مواقع الخطا.
content.en:
The value of consultations

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: One who welcomes various views gets to know where error lies.

title.en: Sayings 174 The worth of getting angry for God

content.ar:
فضل الغضب لله

قال علیه السلام : من احد سنان الغضب لله قوی على قتل اشدا الباطل.
content.en:
The worth of getting angry for God

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: One who sharpens his weapon seeking revenge against those who incur the Wrath of Allah will be strong enough to kill those who are strong in committing falsehood.

title.en: Sayings 175 The fear Treatment

content.ar:
الطریق الی شفا الخوف

قال علیه السلام : اذا هبت امرا فقع فیه، فان شده توقیه اعظم مما تخاف منه.
content.en:
The fear Treatment

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: If you fear doing something, then do it because your fear is greater than the deed itself.

title.en: Sayings 176 The tools of management

content.ar:
اله الریاسه

قال علیه السلام : اله الریاسه سعه الصدر.
content.en:
The tools of management

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: The mechanism whereby one earns mastership is forbearance.

title.en: Sayings 177 How to deal with evil doer

content.ar:
اسلوب مواجهه الاشرار

قال علیه السلام : ازجر المسی بثواب الـمحسن.
content.en:
How to deal with evil doer

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Reprimand the evil doer by rewarding the doer of righteous.

title.en: Sayings 178 The methods of eliminating the evils

content.ar:
الطریق الی قلع الشر

قال علیه السلام : احصد الشر من صدر غیرک بقلعه من صدرک.
content.en:
The methods of eliminating the evils

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Remove evil from the chest of others by eradicating it from your own.

title.en: Sayings 179 Obstinacy and weakness of will

content.ar:
التحذیر من اللجاجه

قال علیه السلام : اللجاجه تسل الرای
content.en:
Obstinacy and weakness of will

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Fanatically insisting on disagreeing poisons one’s view

title.en: Sayings 180 Being greedy and slavery

content.ar:
التحذیر من الطمع

قال علیه السلام : الطمع رق موبد.
content.en:
Being greedy and slavery

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Greed is perpetual bondage.

title.en: Sayings 181 The value of prudence

content.ar:
ثمره الحزم

قال علیه السلام : ثمره التفریط الندامه، وثمره الحزم السلامه.
content.en:
The value of prudence

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: The fruit of carelessness is regret, whereas the fruit of determination is safety.

title.en: Sayings 182 Understanding the role of speech and silence

content.ar:
منزله السکوت و الکلام

قال علیه السلام : لا خیر فی الصمت عن الحکم، کما انه لا خیر فی القول بالجهل.
content.en:
Understanding the role of speech and silence

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Preferring silence over articulating wisdom produces nothing good; likewise is to speak out of ignorance.

title.en: Sayings 183 The reason of disagreement

content.ar:
عله الاختلاف

قال علیه السلام : ما اختلفت دعوتان الا کانت احداهما ضلاله.
content.en:
The reason of disagreement

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: No two cases vary from each other except that one of them is falsehood.

title.en: Sayings 184 Ideological characteristics of Imam(PBUH)

content.ar:
خصایص الامام الاعتقادیه

قال علیه السلام : ما شککت فی الحق مذ اریته.
content.en:
Ideological characteristics of Imam(PBUH)

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Never have I ever doubted the truth whenever it was shown to me.

title.en: Sayings 185 Ideological characteristics of Imam(PBUH)

content.ar:
خصایص الامام الاعتقادیه

قال علیه السلام : ما کذبت ولا کذبت ولا ضللت ولا ضل بی.
content.en:
Ideological characteristics of Imam(PBUH)

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Never have I been told a lie, nor have I been told a lie. Never did I ever stray, nor could any use me to cause others to stray.

title.en: Sayings 186 The future of oppressor

content.ar:
مستقبل الظالم

قال علیه السلام : للظالم البادی غدا بکفه عضه
content.en:
The future of oppressor

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: The oppressor who starts the oppression will tomorrow bite his hand (in regret).

title.en: Sayings 187 The necessity of reminding the Resurrection

content.ar:
ضروره ذکر القیامه

قال علیه السلام : الرحیل وشیک.
content.en:
The necessity of reminding the Resurrection

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Departure (from this world) is nigh.

title.en: Sayings 188 The necessity of acting right

content.ar:
ضروره العمل بالحق

قال علیه السلام : من ابدى صفحته للحق هلک.
content.en:
The necessity of acting right

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: One who openly opposes what is right perishes.

title.en: Sayings 189 The value of patience and the risk of being impatience

content.ar:
فضل الصبر

قال علیه السلام : من لم ینجه الصبر اهلکه الجزع.
content.en:
The value of patience and the risk of being impatience

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: One who is not saved by patience will be finished by impatience.

title.en: Sayings 190 The method for selecting the Caliphate

content.ar:
کیفیه الاختیار الامام

قال علیه السلام :

واعجباه!

اتکون الخلافه بالصحابه

ولاتکون بالصحابها والقرابه؟

قال الرضى :

و روی له شعر فی هذا المعنى، وهو:

فان کنت بالشورى ملکت امورهم

فکیف بهذا والمشیرون غیب؟

وان کنت بالقربى حججت خصیمهم

فغیرک اولى بالنبی واقرب.
content.en:
The method for selecting the Caliphate

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following:

How strange!

Could the caliphate be through the (Prophet's) companionship

but not through (his) companionship and (his) kinship?

Sayyid ar-Radi says the following:

for whom verses of poetry are narrated,has said a couple of lines the gist of which is as follows:

If you through shura took charge of them,

How so since those whose advice is to be sought were absent?!

And if you through kinsfolk argued with the opponent,

Others are more worthy of and nearer than you to the Prophet.

title.en: Sayings 191 The problems of the world

content.ar:
الانسان و مشاکل الحیاه

قال علیه السلام : انما المر فی الدنیا غرض تنتضل فیه المنایا ونهب تبادره المصایب، ومع کل جرعه شرق وفی کل اکله غصص، ولا ینال العبد نعمه الا بفراق اخرى، ولا یستقبل یوما من عمره الا بفراق اخر من اجله فنحن اعوان المنون وانفسنا نصب الحتوف فمن این نرجوا البقا وهذا اللیل والنهار لم یرفعا من شی شرفا الا اسرعا الکره فی هدم ما بنیا، وتفریق ما جمعا؟!
content.en:
The problems of the world

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: One in this life is a target of death, a loot to calamities. With every sip there is hardship, with every morsel there is choking. Whenever a servant of Allah receives a blessing, he bids farewell to another. Whenever he welcomes a new day, he bids farewell for its sake to another. This is so because we are assistants of the fates: Every breath we draw is a target of death; so, how can we hope to last since this night and this day never raise anything in honor except that they are more swift in destroying what they build and scatter what they gather?

title.en: Sayings 192 Warning for gathering property

content.ar:
التحذیر من جمع الاموال

قال علیه السلام : یابن ادم ما کسبت فوق قوتک، فانت فیه خازن لغیرک.
content.en:
Warning for gathering property

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: O son of Adam! Anything you earn beyond your need is stored for others.

title.en: Sayings 193 The best way to utilize the heart

content.ar:
الطریق الامثل لاستعمال القلب

قال علیه السلام : ان للقلوب شهوه واقبالا وادبارا، فاتوها من قبل شهوتها واقبالها، فان القلب اذا اکره عمی.
content.en:
The best way to utilize the heart

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: Hearts have desires, coming or going; so, approach your hearts according to their desires and approaches, for if you force the heart, you will blind it.

title.en: Sayings 194 The necessity of avoiding from anger and revenge

content.ar:
التحذیر من الغضب

قال علیه السلام : متى اشفی غیظی اذا غضبت؟ احین اعجز عن الانتقام فیقال لی: لو صبرت؟ ام حین اقدر علیه فیقال لی: لو عفوت
content.en:
The necessity of avoiding from anger and revenge

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: What shall I satisfy my anger when I am enraged? Is it when I am unable to seek revenge so it will be said to me: “Why did you not have patience?” Or should I do it when I am able, so it will be said to me: “Why did you not forgive?”

title.en: Sayings 195 Avoidance from miserliness

content.ar:
الاجتناب من البخل

وقال علیه السلام وقد مر بقذر على مزبله:

هذا ما بخل به الباخلون

و روی فی خبر اخر انه قال:

هذا ما کنتم تتنافسون فیه بالامس!
content.en:
Avoidance from miserliness

Passing by some garbage, Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

This is some people were too miser to give others.

In another narrative, he is reported as having said,

“This is what you used to compete with each other for!”

title.en: Sayings 196 learning some lessons from wasting properties

content.ar:
العبره من اتلاف المال

قال علیه السلام : لم یذهب من مالک ما وعظک
content.en:
learning some lessons from wasting properties

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: None of your wealth was wasted so long as it provided you with admonishment.

title.en: Sayings 197 The method of psychotherapy

content.ar:
الطریق الی شفا النفس

قال علیه السلام : ان هذه القلوب تمل کما تمل الابدان، فابتغوا لها طرایف الحکمه .
content.en:
The method of psychotherapy

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following: These hearts wear out just as bodies wear out; so, seek for them beautiful pieces of wisdom

title.en: Sayings 198 The necessity of governance

content.ar:
ضروره الحکومه للمجتمع البشری

وقال علیه السلام لما سمع قول الخوارج ـ

لا حکم الا لله ـ

کلمه حق یراد بها باطل.
content.en:
The necessity of governance

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH), having heard the Kharijites say,

“There is no government except Allah’s”, said:

“A true statement intended to reap falsehood.”

title.en: Sayings 199 Blaming the thugs

content.ar:
صفه الغوغا

وقال علیه السلام فی صفه الغوغا

هم الذین اذا اجتمعوا غلبوا،

واذا تفرقوا لم یعرفوا

وقیل: بل قال

هم الذین اذا اجتمعوا ضروا

واذا تفرقوا نفعوا

فقیل: قد علمنا مضره اجتماعهم،

فما منفعه افتراقهم؟

فقال: یرجع اصحاب المهن الى مهنهم،

فینتفع الناس بهم،

کرجوع البنا الى بنایه،

والنساج الى منسجه،

والخباز الى مخبزه.
content.en:
Blaming the thugs

Describing the mobs, Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

They are the ones who overwhelm when they gather

and who are not known.

It is said that he, peace be upon him, rather said,

“They are the ones who do harm when they gather

and they do good when they disperse.”

The Imam (PBUH) was asked, “When understand their mischief when they gather,

but what is the benefit when they disperse?”

The Imam (PBUH) said, “Each one of them returns to his profession,

so people benefit from them,

such as the builder returning to build,

the weaver returns to his craft

and the baker returns to his bakery, and so on.”

title.en: Sayings 200 Criticizing the villains

content.ar:
فضح اهل الغوغا

وقال علیه السلام وقد اتی بجان ومعه غوغا:

لا مرحبا

بوجوه لا ترى الا عند کل سواه.
content.en:
Criticizing the villains

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following when a criminal was brought to him escorted by mobs:

There is no welcome for you here,

O faces that are not seen except when a bad deed is committed.

title.en: Sayings 201 The guardian angels and human protection

content.ar:
الملایکه و حفظ الانسان

وقال علیه السلام : ان مع کل انسان ملکین یحفظانه، فاذا جا القدر خلیا بینه وبینه، وان الاجل جنه حصینه.
content.en:
The guardian angels and human protection

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: With every human being there are two guardian angels. When fate comes, they make room for him; fate is a secure protection.

title.en: Sayings 202 Realism in politics

content.ar:
الامام و النظره السیاسیه

وقد قال له طلحه والزبیر:

نبایعک على انا شرکاوک فی هذا الامر

فقال علیه السلام : لا، ولکنکما شریکان فی القوه والاستعانه،

وعونان على العجز والاود
content.en:
Realism in politics

Talhah and az-Zubair said to the Imam (PBUH),

“We shall swear the oath of allegiance to you provided we are your prtners in this matter (in caliphate).”

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:No, but you both are partners when strength and aid are sought,

and you both are aides against inability and extreme effort.

title.en: Sayings 203 Remembering death and piety

content.ar:
التقوی و ذکر الموت

وقال علیه السلام : ایها الناس، اتقوا الله الذی ان قلتم سمع، وان اضمرتم علم، وبادروا الموت، الذی ان هربتم ادرککم، وان اقمتم اخذکم، وان نسیتموه ذکرکم.
content.en:
Remembering death and piety

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: O people! Fear Allah Who hears you when you speak, Who knows what you hide in your breasts, Prepare yourself to meet death which will overtake you even if you run away which take you when you stay, and which remembers you when you forget about it.

title.en: Sayings 204 Neglecting to gratitude of people

content.ar:
ضروره السعی فی الخیرات

وقال علیه السلام : لا یزهدنک فی المعروف من لا یشکره لک، فقد یشکرک علیه من لا یستمتع بشی منه، وقد تدرک من شکر الشاکر اکثر مما اضاع الکافر، و الله یحب المحسنین.
content.en:
Neglecting to gratitude of people

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Do not be discouraged by ingrates from doing good deeds, for you may be thanked by those who do not benefit from your good deed You may obtain gratitude from a grateful person much more than what someone who hides it has wasted, “... and Allah loves the doers of righteousness.”

title.en: Sayings 205 Unlimited capacity of science

content.ar:
اتساع ظرف العلم

وقال علیه السلام : کل وعا یضیق بما جعل فیه الا وعا العلم، فانه یتسع.
content.en:
Unlimited capacity of science

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Every container becomes straitened by what is put in it except the container of knowledge: It expands

title.en: Sayings 206 The result of patience and endurance

content.ar:
نتایج الحلم

وقال علیه السلام : اول عوض الحلیم من حلمه ان الناس انصاره على الجاهل.
content.en:
The result of patience and endurance

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The first compensation for a clement person for his clemency is that people are his supporters against the ignorant ones.

title.en: Sayings 207 The value to liken to the Clements

content.ar:
فضل التشبه بالحلیم

وقال علیه السلام : ان لم تکن حلیما فتحلم، فانه قل من تشبه بقوم الا اوشک ان یکون منهم.
content.en:
The value to liken to the Clements

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: If you are not clement, then put on the garb of clemency, it is seldom that a man likens himself to a group and does not become almost just like them.

title.en: Sayings 208 The stages of self improvement

content.ar:
مراحل تربیه النفس

وقال علیه السلام : من حاسب نفسه ربح، ومن غفل عنها خسر، ومن خاف امن، ومن اعتبر ابصر، من ابصر فهم، ومن فهم علم.
content.en:
The stages of self improvement

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: One who holds himself to account succeeds. When who overlooks it loses. One who is cautious earns security. One who receives admonishment expands his vision. One who expands his vision gains understanding, and who gains understanding earns knowledge.

title.en: Sayings 209 The news of the appearance of Imam Mahdi (PBUH)

content.ar:
الاخبار بظهور المهدی (عج)

وقال علیه السلام :

لتعطفن الدنیا علینا

بعد شماسها

عطف الضروس على ولدها.

و تلا عقیب ذلک :

«و نرید ان نمن على الذین استضعفوا فی الارض

و نجعلهم ایمه و نجعلهم الو ارثین .»
content.en:
The news of the appearance of Imam Mahdi (PBUH)

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

The life of this world shall be kind to us

after being cruel to us

just as a she-camel that rejects those who milk it but turns with affection to its newborn.

Having said so, the Imam (PBUH) recited the following verse:

“And We wished to be gracious to those who were being oppressed in the land

to make them leaders (in faith) and to make them heirs” (Quran, 28:5).

title.en: Sayings 210 Piety and pious

content.ar:
التقوی و المتقی

وقال علیه السلام : اتقوا الله تقیه من شمر تجریدا، وجد تشمیرا، وکمش فی مهل، وبادر عن وجل و نظر فی کره المویل وعاقبه المصدر، و مغبه المرجع.
content.en:
Piety and pious

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Fear Allah like the one who prepares himself after extracting himself (from worldly affairs) and after getting ready in this way makes effort; then he acts quickly during the period of this life hastens in view of the dangers (of falling into error) and has his eye on proceeding towards the goal, on the end of his journey and on the place of his (eventual) return.

title.en: Sayings 211 Ethical Values

content.ar:
الفضایل الاخلاقیه

وقال علیه السلام : الجود حارس الاعراض و الحلم فدام السفیه و العفو زکاه الظفر و السلو عوضک ممن غدر و الاستشاره عین الهدایه و قد خاطر من استغنى برایه و الصبر یناضل الحدثان و الجزع من اعوان الزمان و اشرف الغنى ترک المنى و کم من عقل اسیر تحت هوى امیر و من التوفیق حفظ التجربه و الموده قرابه مستفاده و لا تامنن ملولا .
content.en:
Ethical Values

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Generosity is the protector of honour; forbearance is the bridle of the fool; forgiveness is the levy of success; disregard is the punishment of him who betrays; and consultation is the chief way of guidance. He who is content with his own opinion faces danger. Endurance braves calamities while impatience is a helper of the hardships of the world. The best contentment is to give up desires. Many a slavish mind is subservient to overpowering longings. Capability helps preservation of experience. Love means well-utilized relationship. Do not trust one who is grieved.

title.en: Sayings 212 Egotism is the enemy of wisdom

content.ar:
افه العقل«عجب»

وقال علیه السلام : عجب المر بنفسه احد حساد عقله.
content.en:
Egotism is the enemy of wisdom

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: A man's vanity for himself is one of the enemies of his intelligence.

title.en: Sayings 213 Patience and satisfaction

content.ar:
الصبر و الرضی

وقال علیه السلام : اغض على القذى و الا لم ترض ابدا .
content.en:
Patience and satisfaction

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Ignore pain otherwise you will never be happy.

title.en: Sayings 214 Humility and prosperity

content.ar:
التواضع و النعم

وقال علیه السلام : من لان عوده کثفت اغصانه.
content.en:
Humility and prosperity

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The tree whose trunk is soft has thick branches.

title.en: Sayings 215 Opposition is the enemy of good thought

content.ar:
افه الرای

وقال علیه السلام : الخلاف یهدم الرای.
content.en:
Opposition is the enemy of good thought

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Opposition destroys good counsel.

title.en: Sayings 216 Power and aggressiveness

content.ar:
القدره و العدوان

وقال علیه السلام : من نال استطال .
content.en:
Power and aggressiveness

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: He who achieves position begins to make wrong use of it.

title.en: Sayings 217 Knowing the human being in change of circumstance

content.ar:
معرفه الرجال فی الحوادث

وقال علیه السلام : فی تقلب الاحوال علم جواهر الرجال.
content.en:
Knowing the human being in change of circumstance

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Through change of circumstances the mettle of men is known.

title.en: Sayings 218 Jealousy damages friendship

content.ar:
الحسد افه الموده

وقال علیه السلام : حسد الصدیق من سقم الموده.
content.en:
Jealousy damages friendship

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Jealousy by a friend means defect in his love.

title.en: Sayings 219 Wishes, damage the thought

content.ar:
المطامع افه العقول

وقال علیه السلام : اکثر مصارع العقول تحت بروق المطامع.
content.en:
Wishes, damage the thought

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Most of the deficiency of intelligence occurs due to the flash of greed.

title.en: Sayings 220 Justice in judgment

content.ar:
العدل فی القضا

وقال علیه السلام : لیس من العدل القضا على الثقه بالظن.
content.en:
Justice in judgment

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: There is no justice in passing a verdict by relying on probability.

title.en: Sayings 221 Painful future of oppressors

content.ar:
مصیر الظالم

وقال علیه السلام : بیس الزاد الى المعاد العدوان على العباد.
content.en:
Painful future of oppressors

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The worst provision for the Day of Judgement is high-handedness over people.

title.en: Sayings 222 Neglecting evil-doers

content.ar:
تغافل الکریم

وقال علیه السلام : من اشرف افعال الکریم غفلته عما یعلم.
content.en:
Neglecting evil-doers

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The highest act of a noble person is to ignore what he knows

title.en: Sayings 223 Modesty and covering defects

content.ar:
فضل الحیا

وقال علیه السلام : من کساه الحیا ثوبه لم یر الناس عیبه.
content.en:
Modesty and covering defects

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Whomever modesty clothes with its dress people cannot see his defects.

title.en: Sayings 224 Some of ethical values

content.ar:
نصایح خالده

وقال علیه السلام : بکثره الصمت تکون الهیبه و بالنصفه یکثر المواصلون و بالافضال تعظم الاقدار و بالتواضع تتم النعمه و باحتمال المون یجب السودد و بالسیره العادله یقهر المناوی و بالحلم عن السفیه تکثر الانصار علیه.
content.en:
Some of ethical values

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Excess of silence produces awe; justice results in more close friends; generosity heightens position; with humility blessings abound in plenty; by facing hardships leadership is achieved; by just behaviour the adversary is overpowered; and with forbearance against a fool there is increase of one's supporters against him.

title.en: Sayings 225 Jealousy and disease

content.ar:
الحسد و المرض

وقال علیه السلام : العجب لغفله الحساد عن سلامه الاجساد.
content.en:
Jealousy and disease

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: It is strange that the jealous do not feel jealous about bodily health.

title.en: Sayings 226 Greed and humiliation

content.ar:
الطمع و الذله

وقال علیه السلام : الطامع فی وثاق الذل.
content.en:
Greed and humiliation

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The greedy is in the shackles of disgrace.

title.en: Sayings 227 The bases of faith

content.ar:
ارکان الایمان

وقال علیه السلام وقد سیل عن الایمان:

الایمان معرفه بالقلب،

واقرار باللسان،

وعمل بالارکان.
content.en:
The bases of faith

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH), was asked about belief (iman) when he said:

Belief means appreciation with the heart,

acknowledgement with the tongue,

and action with the limbs.

title.en: Sayings 228 Values and anti-values

content.ar:
نصایح خالده

وقال علیه السلام : من اصبح على الدنیا حزینا فقد اصبح لقضا الله ساخطا، ومن اصبح یشکو مصیبه نزلت به فقد اصبح یشکو ربه، ومن اتى غنیا فتواضع لغناه ذهب ثلثا دینه، ومن قرا القران فمات فدخل النار فهو ممن کان یتخذ ایات الله هزوا، ومن لهج قلبه بحب الدنیا التاط قلبه منها بثلاث: هم لا یغبه، وحرص لا یترکه، وامل لا یدرکه.
content.en:
Values and anti-values

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: He who is sorrowful for this world is in fact displeased with the dispensation of Allah. He who complains of a calamity that befalls him complains of his Lord (Allah). He who approaches a rich man and bends before him on account of his riches then two-third of his religion is gone. If a man reads the Qur'an and on dying goes to Hell then it means that he was among those who treated Divine verses with mockery. If a man's heart gets attached to the world, then it catches three things, namely worry that never leaves him, greed that does not abandon him and desire which he never fulfils

title.en: Sayings 229 The value of contentment and good humour
title.ar: الحکمة 229 فضل القناعة و حسن الخلق

content.ar:
فضل القناعه و حسن الخلق

وقال علیه السلام :

کفى بالقناعه ملکا

و بحسن الخلق نعیما.

و سیل (ع) عن قوله تعالى

( فلنحیینه حیاه طیبه )

قال:هی القناعه .
content.en:
The value of contentment and good humour

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

Contentment is as good as estate,

and goodness of moral character is as good as a blessing.

Amir al-mu'minin, peace be upon him, was asked about Allah's saying:

We will certainly make him live a life good and pure . (Qur'an, 16:97)

when he said:that means (a life of) contentment.

title.en: Sayings 230 The way of gaining the livelihood

content.ar:
الطریق الی کسب الرزق

وقال علیه السلام : شارکوا الذی قد اقبل علیه الرزق، فانه اخلق للغنى، واجدر باقبال الحظ علیه.
content.en:
The way of gaining the livelihood

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Be a sharer with him who has an abundant livelihood because he is more probable to get more riches and likely to secure an increase of the share therein.

title.en: Sayings 231 Definition of justice and charity

content.ar:
تفسیر العدل و الاحسان

وقال علیه السلام فی قول الله تعالى:

ان الله یامر بالعدل و الاحسان

العدل الانصاف، والاحسان
content.en:
Definition of justice and charity

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said about Allah's saying:

Verily, Allah enjoins justice ('adl) and benevolence (ihsan), (Qur'an, 16:90).

Here 'adl ' means equitableness and ' ihsan ' means favour.

title.en: Sayings 232 The consequence of charity

content.ar:
فضل الانفاق

وقال علیه السلام :

من یعط بالید القصیره

یعط بالید الطویله.

قال الرضى

و معنى ذلک ان ما ینفقه المر من ماله فی سبیل الخیر و البر و ان کان یسیرا

فان الله تعالى یجعل الجزا علیه عظیما کثیرا،

والیدان هاهنا عبارتان عن النعمتین،

ففرق علیه السلام بین نعمه العبد

و نعمه الرب بالقصیره و الطویله؛

فجعل تلک قصیره و هذه طویله،

لان نعم الله ابدا تضعف على نعم المخلوق اضعافا کثیره،

اذ کانت نعم الله اصل النعم کلها،

فکل نعمه الیها ترجع و منها تنزع.
content.en:
The consequence of charity

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

He who gives with his short hand

is given by a long hand.

Sayyid ar-Radi says the following:

The meaning of this saying is that even though what a man spends in charity from his possessions may be small,

yet Allah, the Sublime, gives good reward for it.

And the two hands referred to, here means two favours.

Thus, Amir al-mu'minin has differentiated between the favour of man

and the favour of the Lord (Allah)with short and long

since he has described the first as short and the other as long.

This is because the favours of Allah are ever multiplied manifold to the favours of man

since Allah's favours are the basis of all favours in the sense

that every other favour springs from it and turns to it.

title.en: Sayings 233 Avoiding from beginning the fight

content.ar:
النهی عن المبارزه

و قال علیه السلام لابنه الحسن علیه السلام:

لا تدعون الى مبارزه

و ان دعیت الیها فاجب

فان الداعی الیها باغ

و الباغی مصروع.
content.en:
Avoiding from beginning the fight

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said to his son al-Hasan, peace be upon them both:

Do not call out for fighting

but if you are called to it do respond,

because the caller to fighting is a rebel

and the rebel deserves destruction.

title.en: Sayings 234 Ethical difference between men and women

content.ar:
خصایص الرجال و النسا

وقال علیه السلام : خیار خصال النسا شرار خصال الرجال الزهو والجبن والبخل، فاذا کانت المراه مزهوه لم تمکن من نفسها، واذا کانت بخیله حفظت مالها ومال بعلها، واذا کانت جبانه فرقت من کل شی یعرض لها.
content.en:
Ethical difference between men and women

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The best traits of women are those which are the worst traits of men, namely: vanity, cowardice and miserliness. Thus, since the woman is vain, she will not allow anyone access to herself; since she is miserly, she will preserve her own property and the property of her husband; and since she is weak-hearted, she will be frightened with everything that befalls her.

title.en: Sayings 235 Ethical difference between men and women

content.ar:
علامه العاقل

وقیل له علیه السلام : صف لنا العاقل.

فقال علیه السلام : هو الذی یضع الشی مواضعه.

قیل: فصف لنا الجاهل.

قال: قد فعلت.

قال الرضى :

عنی ان الجاهل، هو الذی لا یضع الشى مواضعه،

فکان ترک صفته صفه

اذ کان بخلاف وصف العاقل.
content.en:
Ethical difference between men and women

It was said to Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH): Describe the wise to us;

and he said: The wise is one who places things in their proper positions.

Then, he was asked: Describe the ignorant to us;

and he said: I have already done so.

Sayyid ar-Radi say the following:

The meaning is that the ignorant is one who does not place things in their proper positions.

In this way, Amir al-mu'minin's abstention from describing was a way of describing him

because the attributes of the ignorant are the opposite of the wise.

title.en: Sayings 236 Understanding the world

content.ar:
علامه العاقل

وقال علیه السلام : والله لدنیاکم هذه اهون فی عینی من عراق خنزیر فی ید مجذوم.
content.en:
Understanding the world

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: By Allah, this world of yours is more lowly in my view than the (left over) bone of a pig in the hand of a leper.

title.en: Sayings 237 Different kinds of worship

content.ar:
انواع العباده

وقال علیه السلام : ان قوما عبدوا الله رغبه فتلک عباده التجار، وان قوما عبدوا الله رهبه فتلک عباده العبید، وان قوما عبدوا الله شکرا فتلک عباده الاحرار
content.en:
Different kinds of worship

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: A group of people worshipped Allah out of desire for reward surely, this is the worship of traders. Another group worshipped Allah out of fear; this is the worship of slaves. Still another group worshipped Allah out of gratefulness; this is the worship of free men

title.en: Sayings 238 The problems of starting a family

content.ar:
مسوولیه العیال

وقال علیه السلام : المراه شر کلها، وشر ما فیها انه لا بد منها.
content.en:
The problems of starting a family

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Woman is evil, all in all; and the worst of it is that one cannot do without her

title.en: Sayings 239 The evilness of laziness and gossiping

content.ar:
الرذایل الاخلاقیه

وقال علیه السلام : من اطاع التوانی ضیع الحقوق، ومن اطاع الواشی ضیع الصدیق.
content.en:
The evilness of laziness and gossiping

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: He who is a sluggard loses his rights and he who believes in the backbiter loses his friend.

title.en: Sayings 240 Prohibition of unlawfully seized

content.ar:
حرمه الغصب

وقال علیه السلام :

الحجر الغصیب فی الدار

رهن على خرابها

قال الرضى :

و یروى هذا الکلام عن النبى (ص)

و لا عجب ان یشتبه الکلامان،

لان مستقاهما من قلیب

و مفرغهما من ذنوب.
content.en:
Prohibition of unlawfully seized

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

One ill-gotten piece of stone in a house

is a guarantee for its ruin.

Sayyid ar-Radi says the following:

In one tradition this saying is attributed to the Prophet(SAAW).

It is no wonder that the two sayings should resemble each other

because they are driven from the same source

and dispersed through the same means.

title.en: Sayings 241 The painful future of oppressors

content.ar:
یوم المظلوم

وقال علیه السلام : یوم المظلوم على الظالم اشد من یوم الظالم على المظلوم.
content.en:
The painful future of oppressors

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The day of the oppressed over the oppressor will be severer than the day of the oppressor over the oppressed.

title.en: Sayings 242 The value of God fearing
title.ar: الحکمة 242 فضیلة الخوف من الله تعالی

content.ar:
فضیله الخوف من الله تعالی

وقال علیه السلام : اتق الله بعض التقى وان قل، واجعل بینک وبین الله سترا وان رق.
content.en:
The value of God fearing

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Fear Allah to some degree (even) though it be little; and set a curtain between you and Allah (even) though it be thin.

title.en: Sayings 243 The correct way of respond

content.ar:
کیفیه الجواب

وقال علیه السلام : اذا ازدحم الجواب خفی الصواب
content.en:
The correct way of respond

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: When replies are numerous the correct point remains obscure.

title.en: Sayings 244 Responsibility for divine blessings

content.ar:
مسوولیه النعم الالهی

وقال علیه السلام : ان لله فی کل نعمه حقا، فمن اداه زاده منها، ومن قصر منه خاطر بزوال نعمته.
content.en:
Responsibility for divine blessings

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Surely in every blessing there is a right of Allah. If one discharges that right Allah increases the blessing, and if one falls short of doing so one stands in danger of losing the blessing.

title.en: Sayings 245 Power and desire

content.ar:
القدره و الشهوات

وقال علیه السلام : اذا کثرت المقدره قلت الشهوه.
content.en:
Power and desire

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: When capability increases, desire decreases.

title.en: Sayings 246 Warning from slipping away of blessings

content.ar:
التحذیر من نفار النعم

وقال علیه السلام : احذروا نفار النعم فما کل شارد بمردود.
content.en:
Warning from slipping away of blessings

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Keep on guard against the slipping away of blessings because not everything that runs away comes back.

title.en: Sayings 247 The result of generosity

content.ar:
افضل الاعمال

وقال علیه السلام : الکرم اعطف من الرحم.
content.en:
The result of generosity

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Generosity is more prompting to good than regard for kinship.

title.en: Sayings 248 Improving optimism

content.ar:
تصدیق حسن الظن

وقال علیه السلام : من ظن بک خیرا فصدق ظنه
content.en:
Improving optimism

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: If a person has a good idea about you make his idea be true.

title.en: Sayings 249 The best acts

content.ar:
افضل الاعمال

وقال علیه السلام : افضل الاعمال ما اکرهت نفسک علیه.
content.en:
The best acts

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The best act is that which you have to force yourself to do.

title.en: Sayings 250 Theology in everyday events

content.ar:
الطریق الی معرفه الله

وقال علیه السلام : عرفت الله سبحانه بفسخ العزایم وحل العقود و نقض الهمم.
content.en:
Theology in everyday events

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: I came to know Allah, the Glorified, through the breaking of determinations, resolving knotty problems and breaching the decisions.

title.en: Sayings 251 Bitterness and sweetness of this world and the hereafter

content.ar:
حلاوه الدنیا و الاخره

وقال علیه السلام : مراره الدنیا حلاوه الاخره، وحلاوه الدنیا مراره الاخره.
content.en:
Bitterness and sweetness of this world and the hereafter

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The bitterness of this world is the sweetness of the next world while the sweetness of this world is the bitterness of the next one.

title.en: Sayings 252 The philosophy of religious precepts

content.ar:
فلسفه الاحکام الاسلامیه

وقال علیه السلام : فرض الله الایمان تطهیرا من الشرک و الصلاه تنزیها عن الکبر و الزکاه تسبیبا للرزق و الصیام ابتلا لاخلاص الخلق و الحج تقربه للدین و الجهاد عزا للاسلام و الامر بالمعروف مصلحه للعوام و النهی عن المنکر ردعا للسفها و صله الرحم منماه للعدد و القصاص حقنا للدما و اقامه الحدود اعظاما للمحارم و ترک شرب الخمر تحصینا للعقل و مجانبه السرقه ایجابا للعفه و ترک الزنى تحصینا للنسب و ترک اللواط تکثیرا للنسل و الشهادات استظهارا على المجاحدات و ترک الکذب تشریفا للصدق و السلام امانا من المخاوف و الامانه نظاما للامه و الطاعه تعظیما للامامه .
content.en:
The philosophy of religious precepts

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Allah has laid down iman (belief) for purification from polytheism; salat (prayer) for purification from vanity; zakat (levy) as a means of livelihood; siyam (fasting) as a trial of the people; hajj (pilgrimage to the House of Allah in Mecca) as a support for religion; jihad (fighting in the way of Allah) for the honour of Islam; persuasion for good (al-amr bi'l-ma`ruf) for the good of the common people; dissuasion from evil (an-nahy 'ani'l-munkar) for the control of the mischievous; regard for kinship for increase of number; revenge for stoppage of bloodshed; the award of penalties for the realization of importance of the prohibitions; the abstinence from drinking wine for protection of the wit; the avoidance of theft for inculcating chastity abstinence from adultery for safeguarding descent; abstinence from sodomy for increase of progeny; tendering evidence for furnishing proof against contentions; abstinence from the lie for increasing esteem for truth; maintenance of peace (salam) for protection from danger; imamah or Imamate (Divine Leadership) for the orderliness of the community and obedience (to Imams) as a mark of respect to the Imamate.

title.en: Sayings 253 How to make an oppressor to swear

content.ar:
کیفیه تحلیف الظالم

و کان علیه السلام یقول: احلفوا الظالم اذا اردتم یمینه

بانه بری من حول الله و قوته

فانه اذا حلف بها کاذبا

عوجل العقوبه

و اذا حلف بالله الذى لا اله الا هو

لم یعاجل

لانه قد وحد الله تعالى
content.en:
How to make an oppressor to swear

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) , used to say: If you want an oppressor to take an oath ask him to swear like this

that he is out of Allah's might and His power,

because if he swears falsely in this way

he will be quickly punished,

while if he swears by Allah Who is such that there is no god but He,

he will not be quickly punished

since he is expressing the Unity of Allah, the Sublime.

title.en: Sayings 254 Initiative to charity

content.ar:
المبادره الی الانفاق

وقال علیه السلام : یا ابن ادم کن وصی نفسک فى مالک و اعمل فیه ما توثر ان یعمل فیه من بعدک .
content.en:
Initiative to charity

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: O son of Adam, be your own representative in the matter of your property and do about it whatever you want to be done with it after your death.

title.en: Sayings 255 Anger and madness

content.ar:
الحده و الجنون

وقال علیه السلام : الحده ضرب من الجنون، لان صاحبها یندم، فان لم یندم فجنونه مستحکم.
content.en:
Anger and madness

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Anger is a kind of madness because the victim to it repents afterwards. If he does not repent his madness is confirmed

title.en: Sayings 256 Jealousy and illnesses

content.ar:
الحسد و الامراض

وقال علیه السلام : صحه الجسد من قله الحسد.
content.en:
Jealousy and illnesses

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Health of body comes from paucity of envy.

title.en: Sayings 257 The way of making others happy

content.ar:
نصایح الخالده

وقال علیه السلام لکمیل بن زیاد النخعی:

یا کمیل، مر اهلک ان یروحوا فی کسب المکارم،

ویدلجوا فی حاجه من هو نایم،

فوالذی وسع سمعه الاصوات

ما من احد اودع قلبا سرورا

الا وخلق الله له من ذلک السرور لطفا

فاذا نزلت به نایبه

جرى الیها کالما فی انحداره

حتى یطردها عنه

کما تطرد غریبه الابل.
content.en:
The way of making others happy

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said to Kumayl ibn Ziyad an-Nakha'i:

O Kumayl, direct your people to go out in the day to achieve noble traits

and to go out in the night to meet the needs of those who might be sleeping,

for I swear by Him Whose hearing extends to all voices

if ever someone pleases another's heart,

Allah will create a special thing out of this pleasing

so that whenever any hardship befalls him

it will come running like flowing water

and drive away the hardship

as wild camels are driven away.

title.en: Sayings 258 Charity and richness

content.ar:
الصدقه و الغنی

وقال علیه السلام : اذا املقتم فتاجروا الله بالصدقه.
content.en:
Charity and richness

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: When you fall into destitution, trade with Allah through charity

title.en: Sayings 259 Understanding the place of loyalty

content.ar:
منزله الوفا

وقال علیه السلام : الوفا لاهل الغدر غدر عند الله، والغدر باهل الغدر وفا عند الله.
content.en:
Understanding the place of loyalty

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said Faithfulness with faithless people is faithlessness with Allah, while faithlessness with faithless people is faithfulness with Allah.

title.en: Sayings 260 Warning about giving time by God

content.ar:
التحذیر من الاملا

وقال علیه السلام :

کم من مستدرج بالاحسان الیه،

ومغرور بالستر علیه،

ومفتون بحسن القول فیه،

ما ابتلى الله سبحانه احدا بمثل الاملا له.

قال الرضى :

و قد مضى هذا الکلام فیما تقدم،

الا ان فیه هاهنا زیاده مفیده
content.en:
Warning about giving time by God

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

There is many a man being gradually brought towards punishment by good treatment with him;

many a man who remains in deceit because his evils are covered;

and many a man who is in illusion because of good talk about him,

while there is no greater ordeal by Allah, the Glorified, than the giving of time.

Sayyid ar-Radi says the following:

This saying has appeared earlier as well

but here it contains a beautiful and useful addition.

title.en: Sayings 261 The innocence of Imam Ali (PBUH)
title.ar: الحکمة 261 مظلوميّة الامام على عليه السلام

content.ar:
مظلومیه الامام على علیه السلام

وقال علیه السلام لما بلغه اغاره اصحاب معاویه على الانبار،

فخرج بنفسه ماشیا

حتى اتى النخیله

فادرکه الناس وقالوا:

یا امیرالمومنین نحن نکفیکهم.

فقال علیه السلام : والله ما تکفوننی انفسکم،

فکیف تکفوننی غیرکم؟

ان کانت الرعایا قبلی لتشکوا حیف رعاتها،

و اننی الیوم لاشکو حیف رعیتی،

کاننی المقود و هم القاده،

او الموزوع و هم الوزعه

فلما قال علیه السلام هذا القول، فی کلام طویل

قد ذکرنا مختاره فی جمله الخطب،

تقدم الیه رجلان من اصحابه فقال

انی لا املک الا نفسی واخی،

فمرنا بامرک یا امیرالمومنین

ننفد له.

فقال علیه السلام :و این تقعان مما ارید
content.en:
The innocence of Imam Ali (PBUH)

When the news of the attack of Mu`awiyah’s men on al- Anbar reached Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH),

he himself came out walking

till he reached an-Nukhaylah

where people overtook him and said:

“O Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib! We are [mighty] enough for [defeating] them.”

He said: You cannot be enough for me against yourselves,

so how can you be enough for me against others?

Before me, the people used to complain of the oppression of their rulers,

but now I have to complain of the wrongful actions of my people;

as though I am led by them and they are the leaders,

or that I am the subject and they are the rulers.

The narrator says: “When Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) uttered this during his long speech,

which we have included in the collection of sermons (No. 27),

two men from among his companions advanced towards him and one of them said:

“‘I rule none except myself and my brother’ (Quran, 5: 25).

So, order us with your command, O Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib

and we will carry it out.”

Thereupon, Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: “How can you two accomplish what I aim at?”

title.en: Sayings 262 Deviants from the right

content.ar:
المنحرفون عن الحق

وقیل: ان الحارث بن حوط اتاه علیه السلام فقال:

اترانی اظن اصحاب الجمل کانوا على ضلاله؟

فقال علیه السلام : یا حارث،

انک نظرت تحتک ولم تنظر فوقک

فحرت

انک لم تعرف الحق

فتعرف من اتاه،

ولم تعرف الباطل

فتعرف من اتاه.

فقال الحارث: فانی اعتزل مع سعید بن مالک وعبد الله بن عمر.

فقال علیه السلام :ان سعیدا وعبد الله بن عمر لم ینصرا الحق،

ولم یخذلا الباطل.
content.en:
Deviants from the right

It is said that al-Harith ibn Hawt came to Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib and said:

“Do you believe that I can ever imagine that the people of Jamal were wrong?”

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: “O al-Harith!

You have seen below yourself but not above yourself,

so you have been confused.

Certainly, you have not known right,

so that you can recognize the righteous.

And you have not known wrong,

so that you can recognize the people who are wrong!”

Then al-Harith said: “In that case, I shall withdraw along with Sa‘d ibn Malik and ‘Abdullah ibn Umar,”

whereupon Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: “Verily, Sa‘d and `Umar have neither sided with right

nor forsaken wrong.”

title.en: Sayings 263 Problems with powerful companionship

content.ar:
صاحب السلطان

وقال علیه السلام : صاحب السلطان کراکب الاسد: یغبط بموقعه، و هو اعلم بموضعه.
content.en:
Problems with powerful companionship

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: One who holds authority is like one who rides a lion he is envied for his status but he well knows it.

title.en: Sayings 264 Benefiting to the survivors of others

content.ar:
فضل الاحسان

وقال علیه السلام : احسنوا فی عقب غیرکم تحفظوا فی عقبکم
content.en:
Benefiting to the survivors of others

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said Do good with the bereaved ones of others so that good is done to your bereaved ones, too.

title.en: Sayings 265 Utterance of the wise persons

content.ar:
کلام الحکما

وقال علیه السلام : ان کلام الحکما اذا کان صوابا کان دوا، و اذا کان خطا کان دا.
content.en:
Utterance of the wise persons

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: When the utterance of the wise is to the point, it serves as a cure but if it is wrong, it acts like an illness.

title.en: Sayings 266 The correct way to response

content.ar:
کیفیه الجواب

وساله علیه السلام رجل ان یعرفه ما الایمان

فقال: اذا کان الغد فاتنی

حتى اخبرک على اسماع الناس،

فان نسیت مقالتی

حفظها علیک غیرک،

فان الکلام کالشارده،

ینقفها هذا و یخطیها هذا.

قال الرضى

و قد ذکرنا ما اجابه به علیه السلام فیما تقدم من هذا الباب،

و هو قوله: الایمان على اربع شعب.
content.en:
The correct way to response

Someone asked Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) to define faith for him.

The Imam said, “Come to me tomorrow

so that I may enlighten you in the presence of all people;

thus, if you forget what I say,

others might retain it.

This is so because an utterance is like a fluttering prey

which may be grappled by someone but missed by others.”

Sayyid ar-Radi says the following:

“We have already stated in the earlier chapter how Imam Ali ibn AbA Talib replied to this man,

namely his saying (No. 31): ‘Faith stands on four pillars, etc.’”

title.en: Sayings 267 Avoidance being greedy

content.ar:
الاجتناب من الحرص

وقال علیه السلام : یابن ادم لا تحمل هم یومک الذی لم یاتک على یومک الذی قد اتاک، فانه ان یک من عمرک یات الله فیه برزقک
content.en:
Avoidance being greedy

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: O son of Adam! Do not inflict the worry of the day that has not yet come on the day which has already come because if that day is in your life, Allah will also bestow its livelihood.

title.en: Sayings 268 Moderation in friendship and enmity
title.ar: الحکمة 268 رعایة العدل فی المودّة و العداوة

content.ar:
رعایه العدل فی الموده و العداوه

وقال علیه السلام : احبب حبیبک هونا ما عسى ان یکون بغیضک یوما ما، و ابغض بغیضک هونا ما عسى ان یکون حبیبک یوما ما.
content.en:
Moderation in friendship and enmity

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Have love for your friend up to a limit, for it is possible that he may turn into your enemy some day. And hate your enemy up to a limit, for it is possible that he may turn into your friend some day.

title.en: Sayings 269 How to deal with the world

content.ar:
انواع الناس فی الدنیا

وقال علیه السلام : الناس فی الدنیا عاملان: عامل عمل فی الدنیا للدنیا، قد شغلته دنیاه عن اخرته، یخشى على من یخلفه الفقر، و یامنه على نفسه، فیفنی عمره فی منفعه غیره و عامل عمل فی الدنیا لما بعدها، فجاه الذی له من الدنیا بغیر عمل، فاحرز الحظین معا، وملک الدارین جمیعا فاصبح وجیهاعندالله لا یسال الله حاجه فیمنعه.
content.en:
How to deal with the world

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: People are two kinds of workers in the world. One is a person who works in this world for this world and his work for this world keeps him unmindful of the next. He is afraid of destitution for those whom he will leave behind but feels safe about it. So, he spends his life seeking the good of others. The other is one who works in this world for what is to come hereafter, and he secures his share of this world without an effort. Thus, he gets both the benefits and becomes the owner of both homes. In this way, he is prestigious before Allah. If he asks Allah anything, He does not deny him

title.en: Sayings 270 The need to preserve the Kaaba's property

content.ar:
القران و اموال الکعبه

وروی انه ذکر عند عمر بن الخطاب فی ایامه حلی الکعبه وکثرته،

فقال قوم:

لو اخذته فجهزت به جیوش المسلمین

کان اعظم للاجر،

وما تصنع الکعبه بالحلی؟

فهم عمر بذلک، وسال عنه امیرالمومنین علیه السلام

فقال: ان القران انزل على النبی صلى الله علیه واله

و الاموال اربعه:

اموال المسلمین

فقسمها بین الورثه فی الفرایض

و الفی فقسمه على مستحقیه،

و الخمس فوضعه الله حیث وضعه،

و الصدقات فجعلها الله حیث جعلها

و کان حلی الکعبه فیها یومیذ،

فترکه الله على حاله،

و لم یترکه نسیانا،

و لم یخف علیه مکانا،

فاقره حیث اقره الله و رسوله.

فقال له عمر: لولاک لافتضحنا

وترک الحلی بحاله.
content.en:
The need to preserve the Kaaba's property

It is related that during the days of (caliph) Umar ibn al- Khattab, the question of the excess of the ornaments of the Ka‘bah was mentioned to him

and some people suggested the following:

“If you raise by it an army of Muslims,

it will be a matter of great reward,

and what will the Ka‘bah do with the ornaments?”

Umar thought of doing so but asked Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) what he thought.

The Imam said:When the Quran was revealed to the Prophet (SAAW),

there were four kinds of property:

One, the property of a Muslim

which he distributed among the successors according to fixed shares.

Second, the tax (fay’) which he distributed to those for whom it was meant.

Third, the one-fifth (khums) for which Allah had fixed the ways of disposal.

Fourth, the amounts of charity (sadaqat) the disposal of which was also fixed by Allah.

The ornaments of the Ka‘bah did exist in those days,

but Allah left them as they were.

He did not leave them by omission,

nor were they unknown to Him.

Therefore, you should keep them where Allah and His Prophet placed them.

Thereupon, Umar ibn al-Khattab said, “If you had not been here, we would have been humiliated.”

He left the ornaments as they were.

title.en: Sayings 271 The right way of judgement

content.ar:
طریق القضا

و روی انه علیه السلام رفع الیه رجلان

سرقا من مال الله،

احدهما عبد من مال الله،

و الاخر من عروض الناس

فقال علیه السلام : اما هذا فهو من مال الله

و لا حد علیه،

مال الله اکل بعضه بعضا،

و اما الاخر فعلیه الحد،

فقطع یده.
content.en:
The right way of judgement

It is related that two persons were brought to Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) once.

They had stolen a public property.

One of them was a slave purchased with public money

and the other had been purchased by someone from among the people.

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said, “As for this one who has stolen the public property

there is no punishment for him,

for it means one who is the property of Allah has taken another property of Allah.

As for the other, he should be punished.”

Consequently, the thief’s hand was cut off.

title.en: Sayings 272 Efforts to remove corruption

content.ar:
الجد فی اقتلاع المفاسد

وقال علیه السلام : لو قد استوت قدمای من هذه المداحض لغیرت اشیا
content.en:
Efforts to remove corruption

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: If my steps acquire firmness out of these slippery places, I will alter several things

title.en: Sayings 273 The necessity of trust in God
title.ar: الحکمة 273 التّوکل علی الله فی الرّزق

content.ar:
التوکل علی الله فی الرزق

وقال علیه السلام : اعلموا علما یقینا ان الله لم یجعل للعبد و ان عظمت حیلته واشتدت طلبته، و قویت مکیدته اکثر مما سمی له فی الذکر الحکیم ولم یحل بین العبد فی ضعفه وقله حیلته وبین ان یبلغ ما سمی له فی الذکر الحکیم، والعارف لهذا العامل به اعظم الناس راحه فی منفعه، والتارک له الشاک فیه اعظم الناس شغلا فی مضره. و رب منعم علیه مستدرج بالنعمى ، و رب مبتلى مصنوع له بالبلوى فزد ایها المستمع فی شکرک، و قصر من عجلتک، و قف عند منتهى رزقک.
content.en:
The necessity of trust in God

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Know with full conviction that Allah has not fixed for any person even though his means (of seeking it) may be great his craving for it intense and his efforts for it acute more livelihood than what has been ordained in the Book of Destiny, Nor does the weakness of a person or the paucity of his means stand in the way between what is ordained in the Book of Destiny and himself. Whoever realizes it and acts upon it is the best of them all in comfort and benefit, while whoever disregards it and doubts it exceeds all men in disadvantages. Very often, a favored person is being slowly driven (towards punishment) through those favors, and very often an afflicted person is being done good through his own affliction. Therefore, O listener, increase your gratefulness, lessen your haste and stay within the bounds of your livelihood.

title.en: Sayings 274 The need for action

content.ar:
ضروره العمل

وقال علیه السلام : لا تجعلوا علمکم جهلا، و یقینکم شکا، اذا علمتم فاعملوا، واذا تیقنتم فاقدموا
content.en:
The need for action

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Do not turn your knowledge into ignorance or your conviction into doubt. When you gain knowledge act (upon it), and when you acquire conviction, proceed (on its basis).

title.en: Sayings 275 Ethical anti-values

content.ar:
الرذایل الاخلاقیه

وقال علیه السلام : ان الطمع مورد غیر مصدر و ضامن غیر وفی و ربما شرق شارب الما قبل ریه، کلما عظم قدر الشی المتنافس فیه عظمت الرزیه لفقده، و الامانی تعمی اعین البصایر، و الحظ یاتی من لا یاتیه.
content.en:
Ethical anti-values

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Greed takes a person to the watering place but brings him back without letting him drink. It undertakes responsibility but does not fulfil it. Often, the drinker gets choked before he quenches his thirst. The greater the worth of a thing yearned for the greater is the grief for its loss Desires blind the eyes of understanding. The destined share will reach him who does not approach it.

title.en: Sayings 276 Avoidance from hypocrisy

content.ar:
الاجتناب من النفاق

وقال علیه السلام : اللهم انی اعوذ بک ان تحسن فی لامعه العیون علانیتی، و تقبح فیما ابطن لک سریرتی، محافظا على ریا الناس من نفسی بجمیع ما انت مطلع علیه منی، فابدی للناس حسن ظاهری، و افضی الیک بسو عملی، تقربا الى عبادک، وتباعدا من مرضاتک
content.en:
Avoidance from hypocrisy

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: O Allah! I seek Your protection from this: that I may appear to be good in the eyes of the people while my inward self may be sinful before You, and that I may guard myself (from sinning) only for show before the people although You are aware of everything about me. Thus, I appear before the people in good shape although my evil acts are placed before You. This means achieving nearness to Your creatures but remoteness from Your pleasure

title.en: Sayings 277 The oath of Imam

content.ar:
حلف الامام علیه السلام

وقال علیه السلام : لا والذی امسینا منه فی غبر لیله دهما تکشرعن یوم اغر ما کان کذا وکذا.
content.en:
The oath of Imam

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: I swear by the One Who let us pass the dark night after which there was a bright day that such and such did not happen.

title.en: Sayings 278 The value of continuation of the action

content.ar:
فضل استمرار العمل

وقال علیه السلام : قلیل تدوم علیه ارجى من کثیر مملول منه.
content.en:
The value of continuation of the action

imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: A small action which is continued with regularity is more beneficial than a long one performed with grudge.

title.en: Sayings 279 Knowing the place of obligatory and recommended action

content.ar:
منزله الواجبات و المستحبات

وقال علیه السلام : اذا اضرت النوافل بالفرایض فارفضوها.
content.en:
Knowing the place of obligatory and recommended action

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: When optional issues stand in the way of obligatory ones, abandon them.

title.en: Sayings 280 Reminding the Resurrection

content.ar:
ضروره ذکر المعاد

وقال علیه السلام : من تذکر بعد السفر استعد.
content.en:
Reminding the Resurrection

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Whoever keeps in view the distance of the journey remains prepared.

title.en: Sayings 281 Wisdom supremacy from eye view

content.ar:
فضل التعقل علی المشاهده

وقال علیه السلام : لیست الرویه کالـمعاینه مع الابصار، فقد تکذب العیون اهلها، ولا یغش العقل من استنصحه.
content.en:
Wisdom supremacy from eye view

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Perception by the eyes is not real observation because the eyes sometimes deceive people; but wisdom does not deceive whomsoever it counsels

title.en: Sayings 282 Selfishness is the enemy of taking advice

content.ar:
افه الموعظه

وقال علیه السلام : بینکم و بین الموعظه حجاب من الغره.
content.en:
Selfishness is the enemy of taking advice

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Between you and preaching there is a curtain of deception.

title.en: Sayings 283 The causes of the  society collapse

content.ar:
علل هلاک الشعب

وقال علیه السلام : جاهلکم مزداد و عالمکم مسوف
content.en:
The causes of the society collapse

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The ignorant among you are excessive in their deeds (without knowledge) while the learned are fall short (in their deeds).

title.en: Sayings 284 Knowledge superiority

content.ar:
فضیله العلم

وقال علیه السلام : قطع العلم عذر المتعللین
content.en:
Knowledge superiority

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Knowledge dispels the excuse of those who advance excuses.

title.en: Sayings 285 The necessity to utilizing the opportunities

content.ar:
اغتنام الفرص

وقال علیه السلام : کل معاجل یسال الانظار وکل موجل یتعلل بالتسویف.
content.en:
The necessity to utilizing the opportunities

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Anyone whom death overtakes early seeks time while the death of anyone who is deferred puts forth excuses for the postponement (of doing good deeds).

title.en: Sayings 286 The end of enjoyments

content.ar:
حلاوه الدنیا و غرورها

وقال علیه السلام : ما قال الناس لشی: طوبى له، الا و قد خبا له الدهر یوم سو.
content.en:
The end of enjoyments

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: For everything to which people say “how good!” there is an evil hidden in this world

title.en: Sayings 287 The problem of understanding fate

content.ar:
صعوبه معرفه القدر

وسیل عن القدر،

فقال: طریق مظلم

فلا تسلکوه،

و بحر عمیق فلا تلجوه،

و سر الله فلا تتکلفوه.
content.en:
The problem of understanding fate

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) was asked about destitution.

He said the following: It is a dark path;

do not tread upon it.

It is a deep ocean; do not dive in it.

And it is the secret of Allah; do not take trouble about (knowing) it.

title.en: Sayings 288 Ignorance and humiliation

content.ar:
الجهل و الاذلال

وقال علیه السلام : اذا ارذل الله عبدا حظرعلیه العلم
content.en:
Ignorance and humiliation

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: When Allah intends to humiliate a person, He denies him knowledge.

title.en: Sayings 289 The perfect model of humanity

content.ar:
خصایص الانسان الکامل

وقال علیه السلام : کان لی فیما مضى اخ فی الله، و کان یعظمه فی عینی صغر الدنیا فی عینه، و کان خارجا من سلطان بطنه فلا یشتهی ما لا یجد و لا یکثر اذا وجد، و کان اکثر دهره صامتا فان قال بذ القایلین ونقع غلیل السایلین، وکان ضعیفا مستضعفا! فان جا الجد فهو لیث غاب و صل واد لا یدلی بحجه حتى یاتی قاضیا، وکان لا یلوم احدا على ما یجد العذر فی مثله حتى یسمع اعتذاره، وکان لا یشکو وجعا الا عند بریه، وکان یقول ما یفعل ولا یقول ما لا یفعل، وکان اذا غلب على الکلام لم یغلب على السکوت، وکان على ما یسمع احرص منه على ان یتکلم، وکان اذا بدهه امران نظر ایهما اقرب الى الهوى فخالفه فعلیکم بهذه الخلایق فالزموها وتنافسوا فیها، فان لم تستطیعوها فاعلموا ان اخذ القلیل خیر من ترک الکثیر.
content.en:
The perfect model of humanity

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said In the past, I had a brother- in-faith and he was prestigious in my view because the world was humble in his eyes, the needs of the stomach did not have a sway over him. He did not long for what he did not get. If he got a thing, he would not ask for more. He remained most of the time silent. When he spoke, he silenced the other speakers. He quenched the thirst of inquirers. He was weak and feeble, but at the time of fighting, he was like the lion of the forest or the serpent of the valley: he would not put forth an argument unless it was decisive He will not abuse anyone in an excusable matter unless he had heard the excuse. He would not speak of any trouble except after its disappearance. He said what he would do and would not say what he will not Even if he could be exceeded in speech, he could not be excelled in silence. He was more eager for keeping quiet than speaking, and if two things confronted him, he would see which was more akin to the longing of the heart and he would oppose it These qualities are incumbent upon you. So, you should acquire them and compete with each other in upholding them. Even if you cannot acquire them, you should know that acquiring a part is better than giving up the whole

title.en: Sayings 290 Responsibility for the blessings

content.ar:
مسوولیه النعم

وقال علیه السلام : لو لم یتوعد الله على معصیه لکان یجب ان لا یعصى شکرا لنعمه.
content.en:
Responsibility for the blessings

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Even if Allah had not warned of chastisement on those who are disobedient to Him, it will be obligatory by way of gratitude for His favors that He should not be disobeyed.

title.en: Sayings 291 The methods offering condolence

content.ar:
طریقه التعزیه

وقال علیه السلام ، وقد عزى الاشعث بن قیس عن ابن له:

یا اشعث، ان تحزن على ابنک

فقد استحقت ذلک منک الرحم،

وان تصبر

ففی الله من کل مصیبه خلف

یا اشعث، ان صبرت

جرى علیک القدر

و انت ماجور،

و ان جزعت

جرى علیک القدر

وانت مازور

یا اشعث ابنک سرک

وهو بلا وفتنه،

و حزنک

وهو ثواب و رحمه.
content.en:
The methods offering condolence

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following to express his condolences to al-Ash‘ath ibn Qays about (the death of) his son:

O Ash‘ath! If you grieve over your son,

certainly it is the consequence of the blood relationship;

but if you endure,

then Allah provides recompense for every affliction.

O Ash‘ath! If you endure even then,

matters will move on as ordained by Allah.

But in that case, you will deserve to be rewarded,

while if you lose patience,

matters will again move as ordained by Allah.

But in this case, you will be bearing the burden (of your sins).

O Ash‘ath! Your son (when he lived) gave you happiness while,

at the same time, he was a trial and a hardship

and (when he died) he caused you to grieve while,

at the same time, he has proved to be a source of reward and mercy for you.

title.en: Sayings 292 The Prophet (SAAW) mourning

content.ar:
عزا رسول الله(ص)

وقال علیه السلام على قبر رسول الله صلى الله علیه واله ساعه دفن:

ان الصبر لجمیل الا عنک،

و ان الجزع لقبیح الا علیک،

و ان المصاب بک لجلیل،

و انه قبلک و بعدک لجلل
content.en:
The Prophet (SAAW) mourning

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following at the grave of the Messenger of Allah (SAAW) at the time of his burial:

Certainly endurance is good except about you.

Fretting is bad except over you.

And the affliction about you is great

while every other affliction before or after t is small.

title.en: Sayings 293 Warning being friend with fools

content.ar:
التحذیر من موده الاحمق

وقال علیه السلام : لا تصحب المایق فانه یزین لک فعله، ویود ان تکون مثله.
content.en:
Warning being friend with fools

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Do not associate with a fool because he will beautify his actions before you and wish that you, too, be like him

title.en: Sayings 294 The distance between the East and West
title.ar: الحکمة 294 المسافة ما بین المشرق و المغرب

content.ar:
المسافه ما بین المشرق و المغرب

وقال علیه السلام وقد سیل عن مسافه ما بین المشرق والمغرب:

مسیره یوم للشمس.
content.en:
The distance between the East and West

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) was asked about the distance between the East and the West when he replied as follows:

A day’s trip for the sun.

title.en: Sayings 295 Recognition of friends and foes

content.ar:
الاصدقا و الاعدا

وقال علیه السلام : اصدقاوک ثلاثه، واعداوک ثلاثه: فاصدقاوک: صدیقک، و صدیق صدیقک، و عدو عدوک واعداوک: عدوک، و عدو صدیقک، و صدیق عدوک.
content.en:
Recognition of friends and foes

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Your friends are three and your enemies are (also) three. Your friends are: your friend, your friend’s friend and your enemy’s enemy. And your enemies are: your enemy, your friend’s enemy and your enemy’s friend.

title.en: Sayings 296 Avoiding from enmity

content.ar:
الاجتناب من العدوان

وقال علیه السلام لرجل راه یسعى على عدو له بما فیه اضرار بنفسه:

انما انت کالطاعن نفسه

لیقتل ردفه
content.en:
Avoiding from enmity

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) saw a man busy against his enemy with what was harmful to his own self as well, so he said:

You are like one who pierces a spear through himself

in order to kill the person sitting behind him.

title.en: Sayings 297 The necessity of learning lesson

content.ar:
فضل الاعتبار

وقال علیه السلام : ما اکثر العبر و اقل الاعتبار!
content.en:
The necessity of learning lesson

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: How many are the objectives of lessons, but how few are those who take lessons!

title.en: Sayings 298 Moderation in enmity

content.ar:
العدل فی الخصومه

وقال علیه السلام : من بالغ فی الخصومه و من قصر فیها ظلم، ولا یستطیع ان یتقی الله من خاصم.
content.en:
Moderation in enmity

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Whoever goes too far in quarreling is a sinner but if one falls short in it, one is oppressed, and it is difficult for a quarreling person to fear Allah.

title.en: Sayings 299 Asking help from the God with prayer

content.ar:
الاستعانه بالصلوه

وقال علیه السلام : ما اهمنی ذنب امهلت بعده حتى اصلی رکعتین و اسال الله العافیه.
content.en:
Asking help from the God with prayer

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: I am not worried about a fault after which I get time to offer prayers in two units (rek‘as) and beg safety from the wrath of Allah.

title.en: Sayings 300 How Allah provides all persons livelihood

content.ar:
عجایب ارتزاق الناس

وسئل علیه السلام :

کیف یحاسب الله الخلق على کثرتهم؟

فقال علیه السلام :کما یرزقهم على کثرتهم.

فقیل: کیف یحاسبهم ولا یرونه؟

قال علیه السلام : کما یرزقهم و لا یرونه.
content.en:
How Allah provides all persons livelihood

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) was asked once:

“How will Allah conduct the accounting of all persons despite their large number?”

He replied: “Just as He provides them livelihood despite their large number.”

Then it was said to Him: “How will He conduct their accounting without their seeing Him?”

He replied: “Just as He provides them with livelihood although they do not see Him.”

title.en: Sayings 301 The sign of wisdom

content.ar:
علامه العقل

وقال علیه السلام : رسولک ترجمان عقلک، وکتابک ابلغ ما ینطق عنک
content.en:
The sign of wisdom

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Your messenger is the interpreter of your intelligence while your letter is more eloquent in expressing your true self

title.en: Sayings 302 Mankind and prayer

content.ar:
الانسان و الدعا

وقال علیه السلام : ما المبتلى الذی قد اشتد به البلا، باحوج الى الدعا من المعافى الذی لا یامن البلا!
content.en:
Mankind and prayer

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The person who is afflicted with hardship is not in a greater need for praying than the one who has been spared affliction but is not immune to it

title.en: Sayings 303 Worldliness value

content.ar:
فضل حب الدنیا

وقال علیه السلام : الناس ابنا الدنیا، و لا یلام الرجل على حب امه
content.en:
Worldliness value

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: People are the progeny of the world and none can be blamed for loving the mother.

title.en: Sayings 304 Compliance of the destitute

content.ar:
اجابه الفقیر

وقال علیه السلام : ان المسکین رسول الله، فمن منعه فقد منع الله، ومن اعطاه فقد اعطى الله.
content.en:
Compliance of the destitute

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The destitute person is a messenger of Allah. Whoever denies him denies Allah, and whoever gives him gives Allah

title.en: Sayings 305 Zealous and leaving adultery

content.ar:
عصمه الغیور

وقال علیه السلام : ما زنى غیور قط.
content.en:
Zealous and leaving adultery

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: A self-respecting man never commits adultery

title.en: Sayings 306 Death is the guard for human

content.ar:
الاجل و حراسه الانسان

وقال علیه السلام : کفى بالاجل حارسا!
content.en:
Death is the guard for human

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The fixed limit of life is enough to remain watchful.

title.en: Sayings 307 The problem of looted property

content.ar:
مشکله الحرب

وقال علیه السلام :

ینام الرجل على الثکل

و لا ینام على الحرب

قال الرضى :

ومعنى ذلک: انه یصبر على قتل الاولاد،

ولا یصبر على سلب الاموال.
content.en:
The problem of looted property

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

A man can sleep over the death of his child

but cannot sleep over the loss of his property.

Sayyid ar-Radi says the following:

“This statement means that a man remains patient about the death of his children

but does not do so at the loss of his property.

title.en: Sayings 308 The result of ancestors' friendship

content.ar:
اثر موده الابا ابنایهم

وقال علیه السلام : موده الابا قرابه بین الابنا، و القرابه الى الموده احوج من الموده الى القرابه.
content.en:
The result of ancestors' friendship

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Mutual affection between fathers creates a relationship between the sons. Relationship is more in need of affection than affection is for a relationship.

title.en: Sayings 309 The thoughts of a believers

content.ar:
ظنون المومنین

وقال علیه السلام : اتقوا ظنون المومنین، فان الله تعالى جعل الحق على السنتهم.
content.en:
The thoughts of a believers

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Be afraid of the thoughts of believers because Allah, the most Exalted One, has placed the truth on their tongues.

title.en: Sayings 310 Trust in the God

content.ar:
الاعتماد علی الله تعالی

وقال علیه السلام : لا یصدق ایمان عبد، حتى یکون بما فی ید الله سبحانه اوثق منه بما فی یده.
content.en:
Trust in the God

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The belief of a person cannot be regarded as true unless his trust in what is with Allah is more than his trust in what he himself has

title.en: Sayings 311 The curse of Imam Ali (PBUH)

content.ar:
لعن انس بن مالک

وقال علیه السلام لانس بن مالک،وقد کان بعثه الى طلحه والزبیر لما جا الى البصره

یذکرهما شییا مما سمعه من رسول الله ص فی معناهما،

فلوى عن ذلک فرجع الیه،

فقال: انی انسیت ذلک الامر :

فقال علیه السلام: ان کنت کاذبا

فضربک الله بها بیضا لامعه

لا تواریها العمامه.

قال الرضى :

یعنی البرص،

فاصاب انسا هذا الدا فیما بعد فی وجهه ،

فکان لا یرى الا مبرقا.
content.en:
The curse of Imam Ali (PBUH)

When Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) came to Basra, he sent Anas ibn Malik to Talhah and az-Zubayr

to make them recall what he himself had heard the Messenger of Allah (SAAW) saying concerning them both

but he avoided doing so. When he came back to Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH ),

he said that he had forgotten that matter.

Thereupon, Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: “If you are speaking a lie,

Allah will afflict you with white spots (leucoderm)

which even the turban may not cover.

Sayyid ar-Radi says the following:

White spot means leucoderma.

After sometime this disease did occur to Anas’s face,

so much so that he was never seen with his face uncovered.”

title.en: Sayings 312 The psychology of worship
title.ar: الحکمة 312 التّعامل مع الفرائض و النوافل

content.ar:
التعامل مع الفرایض و النوافل

وقال علیه السلام : ان للقلوب اقبالا و ادبارا فاذا اقبلت فاحملوها على النوافل، واذا ادبرت فاقتصروا بها على الفرایض.
content.en:
The psychology of worship

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Sometimes the hearts advance [towards their Creator] and sometimes they retreat. When they advance, get them to perform the optional [acts of worship] (as well). But when they retreat, keep them confined to only what is obligatory.

title.en: Sayings 313 The comprehensiveness of the Quran

content.ar:
کمال القران الکریم

وقال علیه السلام : و فی القران نبا ما قبلکم، و خبر ما بعدکم، وحکم ما
content.en:
The comprehensiveness of the Quran

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The Quran contains news about the past foretelling about the future and commandments for the present.

title.en: Sayings 314 How to deal with aggressor

content.ar:
اسلوب مواجهه العدو

وقال علیه السلام : ردوا الحجر من حیث جا، فان الشر لا یدفعه الا الشر.
content.en:
How to deal with aggressor
