Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Throw a stone in return from where one comes to you because evil can be met only with evil.

title.en: Sayings 315 The method of writing

content.ar:
اسلوب الکتابه

وقال علیه السلام لکاتبه عبیدالله بن ابی رافع:

الق دواتک،

واطل جلفه قلمک،

و فرج بین السطور،

وقرمط بین الحروف،

فان ذلک اجدر بصباحه الخط.
content.en:
The method of writing

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said to his secretary ‘Ubaydullah ibn Abu Rafi‘:

Put cotton flake in the ink pot,

keep the nib of your pen long,

leave some space between the lines

and close up the letters

because this is good for the beauty of the writing.

title.en: Sayings 316 Leadership of the believers and wicked persons
title.ar: الحکمة 316 امام المؤمنین و قائد الفجار

content.ar:
امام المومنین و قاید الفجار

وقال علیه السلام :

انا یعسوب المومنین،

والمال یعسوب الفجار.

قال الرضى :

و معنى ذلک ان المو منین یتبعوننی ،

والفجار یتبعون المال،

کما یتبع النحل یعسوبها، و هو رییسها.
content.en:
Leadership of the believers and wicked persons

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

I am the ya‘sAb (leader) of the believers,

while wealth is the leader of the wicked.

Sayyid ar-Radi says the following:

“It means that the believers follow me

while the wicked follow the path that take them to wealth

and riches just as the bees follow their ‘ya`sub’, their leader.”

title.en: Sayings 317 Deviation of Jews

content.ar:
اعوجاج الیهود

وقال له بعض الیهود:

ما دفنتم نبیکم حتى اختلفتم فیه!

فقال علیه السلام له:

انما اختلفنا عنه لا فیه،

ولکنکم ما جفت ارجلکم من البحر

حتى قلتم لنبیکم

اجعل لنا الها کما لهم الهه

فقال انکم قوم تجهلون.
content.en:
Deviation of Jews

Some Jews said to Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH):

“You did not bury your Prophet when you picked up differences about him.”

It is then that Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) replied thus:

“We did not differ about him; we differed after him (i.e. about his succession).

You had not dried up your feet yet, having come out of the river,

when you began asking your Prophet:

‘Make you for us a god as they have gods of their own.’

Said he; ‘Verily you are a people behaving ignorantly’ (Quran, 7: 138).”

title.en: Sayings 318 The courage of Imam

content.ar:
شجاعه الامام علیه السلام

وقیل له: بای شی غلبت الاقران؟

فقال علیه السلام : ما لقیت احدا

الا اعاننی على نفسه.

قال الرضى :

یومى بذلک الى تمکن هیبته فی القلوب.
content.en:
The courage of Imam

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) was asked: “With what did you overpower your adversaries?”

He answered: “Whenever I confronted one of them,

he helped me against himself.”

Sayyid ar-Radi says the following:

“Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) is pointing out his striking of awe in the hearts.”

title.en: Sayings 319 The ominous result of poverty

content.ar:
الاثار الذمیمه للفقر

وقال علیه السلام لابنه محمد بن الحنفیه:

یا بنی، انی اخاف علیک الفقر،

فاستعذ بالله منه،

فان الفقر منقصه للدین،

مدهشه للعقل

داعیه للمقت!
content.en:
The ominous result of poverty

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said to his son Muhammad ibn al-Hanafiyyah:

“O my son! I fear lest destitution overtakes you.

So, you should seek Allah’s protection from it

because destitution is [an indication of] a deficiency in religious beliefs,

perplexity of intelligence,

and it is conducive to hatred of obstinate people.

title.en: Sayings 320 The correct way of asking questions

content.ar:
اهداف السوال

وقال علیه السلام لسایل ساله عن معضله

سل تفقها

ولا تسال تعنتا،

فان الجاهل المتعلم شبیه بالعالم،

و ان العالم المتعسف شبیه بالجاهل المتعنت
content.en:
The correct way of asking questions

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) replied to a man who had asked him a difficult question with the following:

Ask me for understanding

but do not ask for confusion

because the ignorant person who tries to learn is like the learned man,

but the learned man who tries to create confusion is like the ignorant.

title.en: Sayings 321 The leadership's courageous in consultation

content.ar:
عزم القاید فی المشوره

وقال علیه السلام لعبدالله بن العباس، وقد اشار علیه فی شی لم یوافق رایه:

لک ان تشیر علی

و ارى،

فان عصیتک

فاطعنی.
content.en:
The leadership's courageous in consultation

Abdullah ibn Abbas once advised Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) against his views,so the Imam (PBUH) said:

You have only to advise me

but then I have to see (what to do),

and if I act against your advice,

you have to follow me.

title.en: Sayings 322 The need to strengthening the people's morale after the war

content.ar:
تقویه النفوس بعد الحرب

و روی انه علیه السلام لما ورد الکوفه قادما من صفین

مر بالشبامیین،

فسمع بکا النسا على قتلى صفین،

و خرج الیه حرب بن شرحبیل الشبامی

و کان من وجوه قومه،

فقال علیه السلام : ا تغلبکم نساوکم

على ما اسمع؟

الا تنهونهن عن هذا الرنین؟

و اقبل حرب یمشی معه و هو علیه السلام راکب،

فقال له: ارجع

فان مشی مثلک مع مثلی فتنه للوالی

و مذله للمومن.
content.en:
The need to strengthening the people's morale after the war

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) returned to Kufa from Siffin

he passed by the residences of the Shibamites (who belonged to the tribe of Shibam)

and heard their women mourning those killed in Siffin.

At that time, a Shibamite, namely Harb ibn Shurahbil ash-Shibami,went to him

who was one of the nobles of those people.

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said to him, “Do your women have control over you

as regarding the weeping that I hear?

Do you not refrain them from such crying?”

Harb began to walk with him while Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) was on horseback,

so Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said to him: “Get back

because the walking of a man like you with one like me is mischief for the ruler

and a disgrace for the believer.

title.en: Sayings 323 The reasons of deviation of the Kharijite

content.ar:
علل انحراف الخوارج

وقال علیه السلام وقد مر بقتلى الخوارج یوم النهروان:

بوسا لکم، لقد ضرکم من غرکم

فقیل له: من غرهم یا امیرالمومنین؟

فقال: الشیطان المضل،

و الانفس الاماره بالسو،

غرتهم بالامانی،

و فسحت لهم فی المعاصی،

وعدتهم الاظهار،

فاقتحمت بهم النار.
content.en:
The reasons of deviation of the Kharijite

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) passed by the dead bodies of the Kharijites on the day of the battle of Nahrawan and said:

“Woe unto you! You have been harmed by him who deceived you.”

He was asked: “O Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH)! Who deceived them?”

He replied: “Satan, the deceiver,

and the inner self [nafs] that leads one to evil

deceived them through passions.

It made it easy for them to get into sins,

promised them victory

and eventually threw them into the Fire.

title.en: Sayings 324 God-fearing in loneliness situations

content.ar:
تقوی الله فی الخلوات

وقال علیه السلام : اتقوا معاصی الله فی الخلوات، فان الشاهد هو الحاکم.
content.en:
God-fearing in loneliness situations

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Beware of disobeying Allah in solitude because the Witness (of that situation) is also the Judge.

title.en: Sayings 325 Grief in death of Muhammad ibn Abi Bakr
title.ar: الحکمة 325 فضیلة محمد بن ابی بکر

content.ar:
فضیله محمد بن ابی بکر

وقال علیه السلام ، لما بلغه قتل محمد بن ابی بکر

ان حزننا علیه على قدر سرورهم به،

الا انهم نقصوا بغیضا،

ونقصنا حبیبا.
content.en:
Grief in death of Muhammad ibn Abi Bakr

When the news of the killing of Muhammad ibn Abu Bakr reached Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH), he said:

Our grief over him is as great as their (i.e. the enemy’s) joy for it,

except that they have lost an enemy

and we have lost a friend.

title.en: Sayings 326 The deadline of accepting Repentance

content.ar:
مهله التوبه

وقال علیه السلام : العمر الذی اعذر الله فیه الى ابن ادم ستون سنه.
content.en:
The deadline of accepting Repentance

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The age up to which Allah accepts any excuse for a human being is sixty years

title.en: Sayings 327 False victories

content.ar:
الغایب بالشر

وقال علیه السلام : ما ظفر من ظفر الاثم به، و الغالب بالشر مغلوب.
content.en:
False victories

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: One whose sin overpowers him is never victorious, and whoever secures victory by evil means is (in fact) vanquished.

title.en: Sayings 328 The responsibility of the riches

content.ar:
مسوولیه الاغنیا

وقال علیه السلام : ان الله سبحانه فرض فی اموال الاغنیا اقوات الفقرا، فما جاع فقیر الا بما منع به غنی، والله تعالى سایلهم عن ذلک.
content.en:
The responsibility of the riches

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Allah, the Glorified One, has fixed the livelihood of the destitute in the wealth of the rich. Consequently, whenever a destitute remains hungry, it is because some rich person has denied (him his share). Allah, the Sublime, will question them [the rich] about it.

title.en: Sayings 329 No need to apologize

content.ar:
الاستغنا عن العذر

وقال علیه السلام : الاستغنا عن العذر اعز من الصدق به.
content.en:
No need to apologize

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Not to be in need of putting forth an excuse is better than putting forth a true excuse.

title.en: Sayings 330 Responsibility for the blessings

content.ar:
مشوولیه النعم

وقال علیه السلام : اقل ما یلزمکم لله الا تستعینوا بنعمه على معاصیه
content.en:
Responsibility for the blessings

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The least right of Allah on you is that you should not make use of His favors in committing His sins

title.en: Sayings 331 The value of obedience

content.ar:
فضیله الطاعه

وقال علیه السلام : ان الله سبحانه جعل الطاعه غنیمه الاکیاس عند تفریط العجزه
content.en:
The value of obedience

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Allah, the Glorified One, has set obedience to Allah, opportunity for the intelligent when the disabled fail in performing such act.

title.en: Sayings 332 The responsibility of leadership

content.ar:
مسوولیه القاید

وقال علیه السلام : السلطان وزعه الله فی ارضه.
content.en:
The responsibility of leadership

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The sovereign is the watchman of Allah on earth.

title.en: Sayings 333 The psychology of the believer

content.ar:
خصایص المومن

وقال علیه السلام فی صفه المومن:

المومن بشره فی وجهه،

وحزنه فی قلبه،

اوسع شی صدرا،

واذل شی نفسا،

یکره الرفعه، ویشنا السمعه،

طویل غمه، بعید همه،

کثیر صمته، مشغول وقته،

شکور صبور،

مغمور بفکرته،

ضنین بخلته

سهل الخلیقه لین العریکه

نفسه اصلب من الصلد ،

وهو اذل من العبد
content.en:
The psychology of the believer

Describing a believer, Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

A believer has a cheerful face

a sorrowful heart,

a very broad chest (very generous),

and a very humble heart.

He hates high status and dislikes fame.

His grief is long, his courage is far-reaching,

his silence is much and his time is occupied.

He is grateful, enduring,

buried in his thoughts,

sparing in asking his needs,

of a bright demeanor and of a soft temperament.

He is stronger than stone

but more humble than a slave.

title.en: Sayings 334 Reminding the death

content.ar:
ذکر المعاد

وقال علیه السلام : لو راى العبد الاجل ومسیره لابغض الامل وغروره.
content.en:
Reminding the death

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: If a man happens to see the end of (his) life and his final fate, he will begin hating desires and their deception

title.en: Sayings 335 The enemies of properties

content.ar:
افه الاموال

وقال علیه السلام : لکل امرى فی ماله شریکان: الوارث، والحوادث
content.en:
The enemies of properties

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: There are two shares in the property of every person: heirs and accidents.

title.en: Sayings 336 Responsibility for promises

content.ar:
مسوولیه الوعد

وقال علیه السلام : المسوول حر. حتى یعد.
content.en:
Responsibility for promises

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The person who is approached with a request is free till he makes a promise.

title.en: Sayings 337 The necessity for action

content.ar:
ضروره العمل

وقال علیه السلام : الداعی بلا عمل کالرامی بلا وتر.
content.en:
The necessity for action

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Whoever prays but does not exert effort is like one who shoots without a bow-string.

title.en: Sayings 338 Types of science

content.ar:
انواع العلم

وقال علیه السلام : العلم علمان: مطبوع ومسموع ولا ینفع المسموع اذا لم یکن المطبوع.
content.en:
Types of science

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Knowledge is of two kinds: that which is absorbed and that which is just heard. The one that is heard does not give benefit unless it is absorbed.

title.en: Sayings 339 Power and the governance of thoughts

content.ar:
القدره و صواب الرای

وقال علیه السلام : صواب الرای بالدول یقبل باقبالها ویذهب بذهابها
content.en:
Power and the governance of thoughts

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Correctness of decisions goes together with power One emerges with the other’s emergence and disappears when the other disappears.

title.en: Sayings 340 The value of chastity and thanksgiving

content.ar:
فضل العفاف

وقال علیه السلام : العفاف زینه الفقر، والشکر زینه الغنى.
content.en:
The value of chastity and thanksgiving

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The beauty of destitution is chastity and the beauty of riches is gratitude.

title.en: Sayings 341 The painful day of a cruel

content.ar:
یوم الظالم المولم

وقال علیه السلام : یوم العدل على الظالم اشد من یوم الجور على المظلوم!
content.en:
The painful day of a cruel

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The day of justice will be more severe on the oppressor than the day of oppression on the oppressed.

title.en: Sayings 342 Real richness

content.ar:
الغنی الحقیقی

وقال علیه السلام : الغنى الاکبر الیاس عما فی ایدى الناس.
content.en:
Real richness

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The biggest wealth is that one should not have an eye on what others possess.

title.en: Sayings 343 Knowing people and anti-value

content.ar:
معرفه الناس

وقال علیه السلام : الاقاویل محفوظه، والسرایر مبلوه و کل نفس بم ا کسبت رهینه والناس منقوصون مدخولون الا من عصم الله، سایلهم متعنت، ومجیبهم متکلف، یکاد افضلهم رایا یرده عن فضل رایه الرضى والسخط ویکاد اصلبهم عودا تنکوه اللحظه وتستحیله الکلمه الواحده.
content.en:
Knowing people and anti-value

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Utterances are preserved and actions are to be tired. “Every soul, for what it earned, is pawned”(Quran, 74: 38). People are to be made deficient (as regarding their bodies) and meddled with (as regarding their minds) except those whom Allah protects. The ones who inquires from among them aims at confusing, while the one who answers creates hardship. It is possible that the man who has the best views among them will be deviated from the soundness of his thinking by pleasure or displeasure, and it is possible even a man with the best wisdom among them be bothered by a single glance or a single expression may transform him.

title.en: Sayings 344 Paying attention to world mortality

content.ar:
ذکر فنا الدنیا

وقال علیه السلام : معاشر الناس، اتقوا الله، فکم من مومل ما لا یبلغه، وبان ما لا یسکنه، وجامع ما سوف یترکه، ولعله من باطل جمعه، ومن حق منعه، اصابه حراما، واحتمل به اثاما، فبا بوزره، وقدم على ربه، اسفا لاهفا، قد خسر الدنیا و الاخره، ذلک هو الخسران المبین.
content.en:
Paying attention to world mortality

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: O folks! Fear Allah, for there is many a man who aspires for what he does not get, many a builder of a house who does not live to occupy it, and many a collector of that which he shall just leave behind. Possibly he may have collected it wrongfully or by denying one’s right. He acquired it unlawfully and had to bear the weight of sins on its account. Consequently, he returned (from this world) with that weight and came before Allah with sorrow and grief. “He lost this world and (also) the thereafter; that is a loss (which is) manifest” (Quran, 22: 11).

title.en: Sayings 345 One of the ways of being pure

content.ar:
طریق العصمه

وقال علیه السلام : من العصمه تعذر المعاصی.
content.en:
One of the ways of being pure

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Lack of access to sins is also a kind of chastity.

title.en: Sayings 346 The problems of begging

content.ar:
ذله السوال

وقال علیه السلام : ما وجهک جامد یقطره السوال، فانظر عند من تقطره.
content.en:
The problems of begging

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The dignity of your face is solid, but begging dissolves it; therefore, look carefully before whom you dissolve it.

title.en: Sayings 347 Moderation in praise

content.ar:
العدل فی الثنا

وقال علیه السلام : الثنا باکثر من الاستحقاق ملق والتقصیر عن الاستحقاق عی او حسد.
content.en:
Moderation in praise

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: To praise more than what is due is sycophancy; to do it less is either because of inability to speak or of envy.

title.en: Sayings 348 The most serious sin

content.ar:
اشد الذنوب

وقال علیه السلام : اشد الذنوب ما استهان به صاحبه.
content.en:
The most serious sin

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: one who keeps away even from minor sins and, hence, his burden is considered light.

title.en: Sayings 349 The perfect model of a man

content.ar:
نصایح خالده

وقال علیه السلام : من نظر فی عیب نفسه اشتغل عن عیب غیره، ومن رضی برزق الله لم یحزن على ما فاته، ومن سل سیف البغی قتل به، ومن کابد الامورعطب ومن اقتحم اللجج غرق، ومن دخل مداخل السو اتهم، ومن کثر کلامه کثر خطوه، ومن کثر خطوه قل حیاوه، ومن قل حیاوه قل ورعه، من قل ورعه مات قلبه، ومن مات قلبه دخل النار، ومن نظر فی عیوب الناس فانکرها ثم رضیها لنفسه فذاک الاحمق بعینه. والقناعه مال لا ینفد، ومن اکثر من ذکر الموت رضی من الدنیا بالیسیر، من علم ان کلامه من عمله قل کلامه الا فیما یعنیه.
content.en:
The perfect model of a man

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: One who sees his own shortcomings abstains from looking into other’s shortcomings. Whoever feels happy with the livelihood with which Allah provides him does not grieve over what he misses. Whoever draws out the sword of rebellion gets killed by it. Whoever strives without means perishes. Whoever enters the depths gets drowned. Whoever visits places of ill-repute receives blame Whoever speaks more commits more errors Whoever commits more errors becomes shameless. Whoever is shameless will have less fear of Allah. Anyone who decreases his fear of Allah causes his heart to die. One whose heart dies enters the Fire. Whoever observes the shortcomings of others and disapproves of them then accepts them for himself is definitely a fool. Contentment is a capital that does not dwindle. Whoever remembers death much is satisfied with small favors in this world. Whoever knows that his speech is also a part of his action speaks less except where he has some purpose.

title.en: Sayings 350 The psychology of oppressors

content.ar:
نفسیه الظالم

وقال علیه السلام : للظالم من الرجال ثلاث علامات: یظلم من فوقه بالمعصیه، من دونه بالغلبه یظاهرالقوم الظلمه.
content.en:
The psychology of oppressors

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The oppressor among the people has three signs: He oppresses his superior by disobeying him, and his junior by imposing his authority and he tops other oppressors.

title.en: Sayings 351 Being hopeful in adversity

content.ar:
مال الشداید

وقال علیه السلام : عند تناهی الشده تکون الفرجه، وعند تضایق حلق البلا یکون الرخا.
content.en:
Being hopeful in adversity

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: At the extremity of hardship comes relief, and at the tightening of the chains of tribulation comes ease.

title.en: Sayings 352 Moderation in looking after the family

content.ar:
الاشتغال بامور العیال

وقال علیه السلام لبعض اصحابه:

لا تجعلن اکثر شغلک باهلک وولدک

فان یکن اهلک وولدک اولیا الله،

فان الله لا یضیع اولیاه،

وان یکونوا اعدا الله،

فما همک وشغلک باعدا الله؟!
content.en:
Moderation in looking after the family

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following to one of his companions:

Do not devote much of your activity to your wife and children

because if your wife and children are lovers of Allah,

then He will not leave His lovers without caring for them.

And if they are enemies of Allah,

then why should you worry and keep yourself busy about the enemies of Allah?

title.en: Sayings 353 The greatest defect

content.ar:
اکبر العیب

وقال علیه السلام : اکبر العیب ان تعیب ما فیک مثله
content.en:
The greatest defect

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The greatest defect is when you are concerned about the defect (in others) which is already present in yourself.

title.en: Sayings 354 How to congratulate on the birth of a child

content.ar:
طریق التهنیه للولد

وهنا بحضرته علیه السلام رجل رجلا بغلام ولد له فقال له:

لیهنیک الفارس

فقال علیه السلام : لا تقل ذلک،

ولکن قل: شکرت الواهب،

وبورک لک فی الموهوب،

وبلغ اشده،

ورزقت بره.
content.en:
How to congratulate on the birth of a child

Someone congratulated another person in the presence of Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) on the birth of a son saying:

“Congratulations for getting a rider of horses”

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH), said: Do not say so;

but say: You have an occasion to be grateful to Allah, the Giver,

and be blessed with what you have been given.

May he attain full life,

And may you be blessed with His devotion.

title.en: Sayings 355 Avoiding from being luxury

content.ar:
علامه الغنی

وبنى رجل من عماله بنا فخما فقال علیه السلام :

اطلعت الورق رووسها!

ان البنا لیصف عنک الغنى.
content.en:
Avoiding from being luxury

One of the officers of Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) built a stately house about which Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

These are silver coins showing their faces.

Certainly, this house speaks of your riches.

title.en: Sayings 356 The power of God in providing livelihood
title.ar: الحکمة 356 قدرة الله فی الرزق و الاجل

content.ar:
قدره الله فی الرزق و الاجل

وقیل له علیه السلام :

لو سد على رجل باب بیته، وترک فیه،

من این کان یاتیه رزقه؟

فقال علیه السلام : من حیث کان یاتیه اجله.
content.en:
The power of God in providing livelihood

It was said to Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) :

“If a man is left in his house and the door is closed,

from where will his livelihood reach him?”

He replied: “From whatever way his death reaches him.”

title.en: Sayings 357 The way of consoling

content.ar:
طریقه التعزیه

وعزى علیه السلام قوما عن میت

فقال:ان هذا الارلیس بکم بدا،

ولا الیکم انتهى،

وقد کان صاحبکم هذا یسافر،

فعدوه فی بعض اسفاره،

فان قدم علیکم

والا قدمتم علیه.
content.en:
The way of consoling

Offering condolences to people one of whom had just died,

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: This thing has not started with you

nor does it end with you.

This fellow of yours was used to journeyin

and therefore, it is better to think of him as still journeying.

Either he will rejoin you

or you will rejoin him

title.en: Sayings 358 The responsibility for blessings

content.ar:
مسوولیه النعم

وقال علیه السلام : ایها الناس، لیرکم الله من النعمه وجلین کما یراکم من النقمه فرقین انه من وسع علیه فی ذات یده فلم یر ذلک استدراجا فقد امن مخوفا، ومن ضیق علیه فی ذات یده فلم یر ذلک اختبارا فقد ضیع مامولا.
content.en:
The responsibility for blessings

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: O people! Let Allah see you fearing at the time of happiness just as you fear Him at the time of distress. Certainly, whoever is given ease (of life) and does not consider it as a means of slow approach to tribulation (wrongly) considers himself as safe against what is to be feared while whoever is afflicted with straitened circumstances but does not perceive them to be a trial loses to a coveted reward.

title.en: Sayings 359 How to self-regulate

content.ar:
تربیه النفس

وقال علیه السلام : یا اسرى الرغبه اقصروا فان المعرج على الدنیا لا یروعه منها الا صریف انیاب الحدثان ایها الناس، تولوا من انفسکم تادیبها، واعدلوا بها عن ضراوه عاداتها
content.en:
How to self-regulate

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: O slaves of desires! Cut them short because whoever leans on the world gets nothing out of it except the pain of hardships. O people! Take upon yourselves to train your own souls and turn away from the dictates of your natural inclinations.

title.en: Sayings 360 Avoiding from suspicion

content.ar:
التحذیر من سو الظن

وقال علیه السلام : لا تظنن بکلمه خرجت من احد سوا، وانت تجد لها فی الخیر محتملا.
content.en:
Avoiding from suspicion

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Do not regard an expression uttered by any person as evil if you can find it capable of bearing some good.

title.en: Sayings 361 How to ask God
title.ar: الحکمة 361 طریقة السؤال من الله تعالی

content.ar:
طریقه السوال من الله تعالی

وقال علیه السلام : اذا کانت لک الى الله سبحانه حاجه فابدا بمساله الصلاه على النبی صلى الله علیه واله ، ثم سل حاجتک، فان الله اکرم من ان یسال حاجتین فیقضی احداهما ویمنع الاخرى.
content.en:
How to ask God

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: If you have a need from Allah, the Glorified One, then begin by seeking Allah’s blessing on His Messenger, may Allah bless him and his descendants, then ask your need, because Allah is too generous to accept one (seeking His blessing on His Messenger) of the two requests made to Him while denying the other

title.en: Sayings 362 Avoiding from controversy and conflict

content.ar:
الاجتناب من المجادله

وقال علیه السلام : من ضن بعرضه فلیدع المرا.
content.en:
Avoiding from controversy and conflict

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Whoever is jealous of his esteem should keep from quarreling

title.en: Sayings 363 A sign of being foolish

content.ar:
علامه الاحمق

وقال علیه السلام : من الخرق المعاجله قبل الامکان، والاناه بعد الفرصه .
content.en:
A sign of being foolish

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: It is folly to make haste before the proper time or to delay after a proper opportunity.

title.en: Sayings 364 Asking situations

content.ar:
منزله السوال

وقال علیه السلام : لا تسال عما لا یکون، ففی الذی قد کان لک شغل.
content.en:
Asking situations

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Do not ask about things which may not happen because you have enough to worry about with what already happens.

title.en: Sayings 365 Ethical values

content.ar:
الفضایل الاخلاقیه

وقال علیه السلام : الفکر مراه صافیه، والاعتبار منذر ناصح، وکفى ادبا لنفسک تجنبک ما کرهته لغیرک.
content.en:
Ethical values

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Imagination is a clear mirror, and the taking of lessons (from things around you) provides warning and counsel. It is enough for improving yourself that you should avoid what you consider as bad in others.

title.en: Sayings 366 The coordination of science and practice

content.ar:
وحده العلم و العمل

وقال علیه السلام : العلم مقرون بالعمل فمن علم عمل، والعلم یهتف بالعمل فان اجابه والا ارتحل.
content.en:
The coordination of science and practice

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Knowledge is associated with action. Therefore, whoever knows should act [upon it] because knowledge calls for action. If there is a response, well and good; otherwise, it (i.e. knowledge) departs from him.

title.en: Sayings 367 How to deal with the world

content.ar:
اسلوب مواجهه الدنیا

وقال علیه السلام : یا ایها الناس، متاع الدنیا حطام موبى فتجنبوا مرعاه قلعتها احظی من طمانینتها وبلغتها ازکى من ثروتها، حکم على مکثر منها بالفاقه واعین من غنی عنها بالراحه، من راقه زبرجها اعقبت ناظریه ومن استشعرالشغف بها ملات ضمیره اشجانا لهن رقص على سویدا قلبه هم یشغله، وغم یحزنه، کذلک حتى یوخذ بکظمه فیلقى منقطعا ابهراه، هینا على الله فناوه، وعلى الاخوان وانما ینظر المومن الى الدنیا بعین الاعتبار ویقتات منها ببطن ویسمع فیها باذن المقت والابغاض ان قیل اثرى قیل اکدى وان فرح له بالبقا حزن له هذا ولم یاتهم یوم فیه یبلسون.
content.en:
How to deal with the world

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: O people! The wealth of this world is broken into sorts that may bring an epidemic; therefore, keep off this grazing land. Leaving it is a greater favor than peacefully staying in it, and its part which suffices for subsistence is more blissful than its riches. Destitution has been ordained for those who are rich here, While comfort has been designated for those who keep away from it. If a person is attracted by its dazzle, it blinds both his eyes. And if a person acquires eagerness towards it it fills his heart with grief which keeps alternating in the black part of his heart, some grief worrying him and another giving him pain. This goes on till the suffocation of death overtakes him. He is flung in the open while both his arteries are severed. It is easy for Allah to cause him to die and for his comrades to put him in the grave The believer sees the world with eyes that derive instruction and takes from it food enough for his barest needs. He hears in it with ears of hatred and enmity [towards this world]. If it is said (about someone) that he has become rich, it is also said that he has turned destitute. And if pleasure is felt on one’s own living, grief is felt over his death. This is the status, although the day has not yet approached when they will be disheartened.

title.en: Sayings 368 The philosophy of punishment and reward

content.ar:
فلسفه العذاب و الثواب

وقال علیه السلام : ان الله سبحانه وضع الثواب على طاعته، والعقاب على معصیته، ذیاده لعباده عن نقمته، و حیاشه لهم الى جنته.
content.en:
The philosophy of punishment and reward

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Allah, the Glorified One, has laid down rewards for obedience to Him and punishment for committing sins against Him in order to save men from His chastisement and to drive them towards Paradise.

title.en: Sayings 369 News from future

content.ar:
الاخبار عن المستقبل

وقال علیه السلام : یاتی على الناس زمان لا یبقى فیهم من القران الا رسمه، ومن الاسلام الا اسمه، مساجدهم یومیذ عامره من البنى، خراب من الهدى، سکانها وعمارها شر اهل الارض، منهم تخرج الفتنه، والیهم تاوی یردون من شذ عنها ویسوقون من تاخر عنها یقول الله تعالى: فبی حلفت لابعثن على اولیک فتنه اترک الحلیم فیها حیران، وقد فعل، ونحن نستقیل الله عثره الغفله.
content.en:
News from future

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: A time will come when nothing will remain of the Qur’an except its writing and nothing of Islam except its name. The mosques in such days will be busy with regard to construction but desolate with regard to guidance. Those staying in them and those visiting them will be the worst of all people on earth. From them, mischief will spring up and, toward them, all wrong will turn. If anyone isolates himself from it (mischief), they will fling him back into it. And if anyone steps back from it, they will push him towards it. Says Allah, the Glorified One, (in one hadith qudsi, i.e. the tradition in which Allah Himself speaks): I swear by Myself that I shall send upon them an evil wherein the one who endures will be bewildered,” and He will do so. We seek Allah’s pardon against stumbling through neglect.

title.en: Sayings 370 The necessity of virtue

content.ar:
ضروره التقوی

وروی انه علیه السلام قلما اعتدل به المنبر

الا قال امام خطبته:

ایها الناس، اتقوا الله،

فما خلق امر عبثا فیلهو

ولا ترک سدى فیلغو

وما دنیاه التی تحسنت له

بخلف من الاخره التی قبحها سو النظر عنده،

وما المغرور

الذی ظفر من الدنیا

باعلى همته کالاخر الذی ظفر من الاخره بادنى سهمته.
content.en:
The necessity of virtue

It is related that seldom did Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) ascend the pulpit

without uttering the following statement before starting his sermon:

O people! Fear Allah,

for man has not been created for naught so that he may waste himself,

nor has he been left without anyone caring for them so that he may commit foolish acts.

This world, which appears to him to be beautiful

cannot be the replacement for the next which appears in his eyes to be bad,

nor is the vain person,

who is successful in this world,

is sure that he will prosper in the next even to a small extent.

title.en: Sayings 371 The great ethical values

content.ar:
نصایح خالده

وقال علیه السلام : لا شرف اعلى من الاسلام، ولا عز اعز من التقوى، ولا معقل احصن من الورع، لا شفیع انجح من التوبه، ولا کنز اغنى من القناعه، ولا مال اذهب للفاقه من الرضى بالقوت، ومن اقتصر على بلغه الکفاف فقد انتظم الراحه وتبوا خفض الدعه والرغبه مفتاح النصب، ومطیه التعب، والحرص والکبر والحسد دواع الى التقحم فی الذنوب، والشر جامع مساوى العیوب.
content.en:
The great ethical values

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: There is no distinction higher than Islam, no honor more honorable than fear of Allah, no asylum better than self-restraint, no intercessor more effective than repentance no treasure more precious than contentment, and no wealth is a bigger remover of destitution than satisfaction with mere subsistence. Whoever confines himself to what is just enough for maintenance achieves comfort and prepares his abode in ease. Desire is the key of grief and the conveyance of distress. Greed, vanity and jealousy are incentives to falling into sins and mischief-making, the collection of all bad habits

title.en: Sayings 372 The mainstay of religion and the world

content.ar:
قوام الدین و الدنیا

وقال علیه السلام لجابر بن عبدالله الانصاری:

یا جابر، قوام الدین و الدنیا باربعه:

عالم مستعمل علمه،

وجاهل لا یستنکف ان یتعلم،

وجواد لا یبخل بمعروفه،

وفقیر لا یبیع اخرته بدنیاه؛

فاذا ضیع العالم علمه

استنکفال جاهل ان یتعلم،

واذا بخل الغنی بمعروفه

باع الفقیر اخرته بدنیاه

یا جابر، من کثرت نعم الله علیه

کثرت حوایج الناس الیه،

فمن قام لله فیها بما یجب

عرضها للدوام والبقا،

ومن لم یقم لله فیها بما یجب

عرضها للزوال والفنا.
content.en:
The mainstay of religion and the world

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said the following to Jabir ibn ‘Abdullah al-Ansari:

O Jabir! The mainstay of religion and the world are four persons:

The scholar who acts upon his knowledge,

the ignorant person who does not feel ashamed of learning,

the generous person who is not niggardly in his favors

and the destitute who does not sell his next life for his worldly benefits.

Consequently, when the scholar wastes his knowledge,

the ignorant feels too ashamed to learn,

and when the generous is niggardly with his favors,

the destitute sells his next life for the worldly benefits.

O Jabir! If favors of Allah abound on a person,

the people’s needs toward him also abound.

Therefore, whoever fulfills for Allah all that is obligatory on him in this regard

will preserve them (Allah’s favors) in continuance and perpetuity,

while whoever does not fulfil those obligations

will expose them to decay and destruction.

title.en: Sayings 373 The steps of the persuasion for good and dissuasion from evil,
title.ar: الحکمة 373 مراحل الامر بالمعروف و النّهی عن المنکر

content.ar:
مراحل الامر بالمعروف و النهی عن المنکر

وروى ابن جریر الطبری فی تاریخه عن عبدالرحمن بن ابی لیلى الفقیه

وکان ممن خرج لقتال الحجاج مع ابن الاشعث

انه قال فیما کان یحض به الناس على الجهاد:

انی سمعت علیا -

رفع الله درجته فی الصالحین

و اثابه ثواب الشهدا و الصدیقین

یقول یوم لقینا اهل الشام :

ایها المومنون، انه من راى عدوانا یعمل به

ومنکرا یدعى الیه،

فانکره بقلبه فقد سلم

وبرى

ومن انکره بلسانه فقد اجر

وهو افضل من صاحبه،

ومن انکره بالسیف

لتکون کلمه الله هی العلیا

وکلمه الظالمین السفلى،

فذلک الذی اصاب سبیل الهدى،

وقام على الطریق،

ونور فی قلبه الیقین.
content.en:
The steps of the persuasion for good and dissuasion from evil,

Ibn Jarir at-Tabari has, in his History related from ‘Abd ar-Rahman ibn Abu Layla, al-faqih,

who was one of those who had risen with Ibn al-Ash‘ath to fight al-Hajjaj,

that he (Ibn Abu Layla) was exhorting people to carry out jihad by recalling the following:

I heard Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib(PBUH) -

may Allah exalt his rank among the righteous

and may He reward him with the reward of martyrs and men of truth

On the occasion of the encounter with the people of Syria - saying :

‘O believers, whoever observes excesses being committed

and people being called towards evil

and disapproves it with his heart is safe

and free from responsibility for it,

and whoever disapproves of it with his tongue will be rewarded

and he is in a higher status than the former,

but whoever disapproves it with his sword

in order that the word of Allah may remain superior

and the word of the oppressors may remain inferior,

catches hold of the path of guidance

and stands on the right way,

while his heart is lit with conviction.’”

title.en: Sayings 374 Steps of the persuasion for good and dissuasion from evil,
title.ar: الحکمة 374 مراتب الامر بالمعروف و النّهی عن المنکر

content.ar:
مراتب الامر بالمعروف و النهی عن المنکر

وقد قال علیه السلام فی کلام غیر هذا یجری هذا المجرى:

فمنهم المنکر للمنکر

بیده ولسانه وقلبه

فذلک المستکمل لخصال الخیر،

ومنهم المنکر بلسانه وقلبه

والتارک بیده

فذلک متمسک بخصلتین من خصال الخیر ومضیع خصله،

ومنهم المنکر بقلبه

والتارک بیده ولسانه

فذلک الذی ضیع اشرف الخصلتین من الثلاث وتمسک بواحده،

ومنهم تارک لانکار المنکر بلسانه وقلبه ویده

فذلک میت الاحیا

وما اعمال البر کلها والجهاد فی سبیل الله،

عند الامر بالمعروف والنهی عن المنکر،

الا کنفثه فی بحر لجی

وان الامر بالمعروف والنهی عن المنکر لا یقربان من اجل،

ولا ینقصان من رزق،.

وافضل من ذلک کله کلمه عدل عند امام جایر
content.en:
Steps of the persuasion for good and dissuasion from evil,

Another saying in the same strain runs as follows:

So..., among them (the Muslim community) there is one who disapproves evil

with his hand, tongue and heart.

This man has perfectly attained the virtuous habits.

And among them there is one who disapproves evil with his tongue and heart

but not with his hand.

This man has attained only two virtuous habits but lacks one.

And among them there is the third who disapproves evil with his heart

but not with his tongue and hand.

This is the one who lacks the two better qualities out of the three and holds only one.

Then, among them there is also one who does not disprove evil with his tongue, heart or hand.

He is just a dead man among the living.

All the virtuous acts, including waging a war in the way of Allah,

as compared to the persuasion for good and dissuasion from evil,

are just like spitting in the deep ocean [i.e. of no consequence].

The acts of persuasion for good and dissuasion from evil do not bring death nearer,

nor do they lessen the livelihood.

And better than all this is to utter a just expression before a tyrannical ruler

title.en: Sayings 375 Stages of Jihad

content.ar:
مراحل الجهاد

وعن ابی جحیفه قال:

سمعت امیرالمومنین علیه السلام یقول:

اول ما تغلبون علیه من الجهاد الجهاد بایدیکم،

ثم بالسنتکم، ثم بقلوبکم؛

فمن لم یعرف بقلبه معروفا،

ولم ینکر منکرا، قلب

فجعل اعلاه اسفله،

واسفله اعلاه.
content.en:
Stages of Jihad

The following is related from Abu Juhayfah who said,

“I heard Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) saying:

The first fighting with which you will be overpowered is fighting with the hands.

Thereafter, you will fight with your tongues then with your hearts.

Consequently, whoever does not recognize virtue with his heart

or does not disprove evil will be turned upside down.

Thus, his upside will be turned downwards

and his low side will be turned upwards.

title.en: Sayings 376 The future of right and wrong things

content.ar:
مستقبل الحق و الباطل

وقال علیه السلام : ان الحق ثقیل مری وان الباطل خفیف وبی .
content.en:
The future of right and wrong things

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Certainly, right is weighty and wholesome while wrong is light and epidemic.

title.en: Sayings 377 Fear and hope

content.ar:
الخوف و الرجا

وقال علیه السلام : لا تامنن. على خیر هذه الامه عذاب الله، لقوله تعالى: فلا یامن مکر الله الا القوم الخاسرون و لا تیاسن لشر هذه الامه من روح الله لقوله سبحانه: انه لا ییاس من روح الله الا القوم الکافرون.
content.en:
Fear and hope

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Do not feel safe from the punishment of Allah even about the best man in the whole community because Allah, the Sublime, says: “But none feels secure from the plan of Allah save the people (who are the) losers”(Quran,7: 99). Again, do not lose hope even for the worst man of the community because Allah, the Sublime One, says: “Verily, none despair from Allah’s mercy save the disbelieving people” (Quran, 12: 87).

title.en: Sayings 378 The ominous effects of miserliness

content.ar:
نتایج السو للبخل

وقال علیه السلام : البخل جامع لمساوى العیوب، وهو زمام یقاد به الى کل سو.
content.en:
The ominous effects of miserliness

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Miserliness contains all other evil vices and is the rein with which one can be led to every evil.

title.en: Sayings 379 Types of livelihood

content.ar:
الوان الرزق

وقال علیه السلام :

یابن ادم،

الرزق رزقان:

رزق تطلبه،

ورزق یطلبک،

فان لم تاته اتاک،

فلا تحمل هم سنتک على هم یومک!

کفاک کل یوم ما فیه،

فان تکن السنه من عمرک

فان الله تعالى سیوتیک فی کل غد جدید ماقسم لک،

وان لم تکن السنه من عمرک

فما تصنع بالهم لما لیس لک،

ولن یسبقک الى رزقک طالب،

ولن یغلبک علیه غالب،

ولن یبطى عنک ما قد قدر لک.

قال الرضى :

وقد مضى هذا الکلام فیما تقدم من هذا الباب،

الا انه ها هنا اوضح واشرح،

فلذلک کررناه على القاعده المقرره فی اول هذا الکتاب.
content.en:
Types of livelihood

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

O son of Adam!

Livelihood is of two kinds:

The livelihood which you seek

and the livelihood which seeks you;

if you do not reach it, it will reach you.

Therefore, do not turn your one day’s worry into a year’s worry.

Whatever you get every day should be enough for you for the day.

If you have a whole year of your life,

even then Allah, the Sublime, will give you every next day what He has destined as your share.

If you do not have a year in your life-span,

then why should you worry for what is not for you?

No seeker will reach your livelihood before you,

nor will anyone overpower you in the matter of livelihood.

Likewise, whatever has been destined as your share will not be delayed for you

Sayyid ar-Radi says the following:

“This statement has already appeared elsewhere in this Chapter

except that here it is clearer and more detailed.

This is why we have repeated it according to the principle laid down in the beginning of the book.”

title.en: Sayings 380 The necessity to reminiscing the death

content.ar:
ذکر الموت

وقال علیه السلام : رب مستقبل یوما لیس بمستدبره ومغبوط فی اول لیله قامت بواکیه فی اخره
content.en:
The necessity to reminiscing the death

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Many a man faces a day after which he finds no day, and many a man is in an enviable status in the earlier part of the night but is mourned by wailing women in its later part.

title.en: Sayings 381 To keep secret

content.ar:
حفظ السر

وقال علیه السلام : الکلام فی وثاقکما لم تتکلم به، فاذا تکلمت به صرت فی وثاقه، فاخزن لسانک کما تخزن ذهبک وورقک فرب کلمه سلبت نعمه وجلبت نقمه.
content.en:
To keep secret

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Words are in your control till you have not uttered them. But when you have spoken them, you are under their control. Therefore, guard your tongue as you guard your gold and silver, for often one expression snatches away a blessing and invites a penalty

title.en: Sayings 382 The value of silence

content.ar:
فضل السکوت

وقال علیه السلام : لا تقل ما لا تعلم، بل لا تقل کل ما تعلم، فان الله سبحانه قد فرض على جوارحک کلها فرایض یحتج بها علیک یوم القیامه.
content.en:
The value of silence

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Do not say what you do not know; rather, do not say all that you know because Allah has laid down some obligations for all your limbs by means of which He will put forth arguments against you on the Day of Judgement.

title.en: Sayings 383 Thraldom

content.ar:
السعی فی طاعه الله

وقال علیه السلام : احذر ان یراک الله عند معصیته، ویفقدک عند طاعته فتکون من الخاسرین، واذا قویت فاقو على طاعه الله، واذا ضعفت فاضعف عن معصیه الله
content.en:
Thraldom

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Fear lest Allah should see you committing His sins or misses you when it is time to obey Him and, as a result, you will become a loser. Therefore, when you are strong, be strong in obeying Allah and when you are weak, be weak in committing sins against Allah.

title.en: Sayings 384 The correct way of living

content.ar:
نصایح خالده

وقال علیه السلام : الرکون الى الدنیا مع ما تعاین منها جهل، والتقصیر فی حسن العمل اذا وثقت بالثواب علیه غبن والطمانینه الى کل احد قبل الاختبار عجز.
content.en:
The correct way of living

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Leaning towards this world despite what you see of it is a folly, and lagging behind in doing good deeds when you are convinced of good reward for them is an obvious loss, while trusting in everyone before trying is a weakness.

title.en: Sayings 385 Humbleness of the world

content.ar:
هوان الدنیا

وقال علیه السلام : من هوان الدنیا على الله انه لا یعصى الا فیها، ولا ینال ما عنده الا بترکها.
content.en:
Humbleness of the world

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: It is (the proof of the) humbleness of the world before Allah that He is disobeyed only herein and His favors cannot be achieved except by renouncing it

title.en: Sayings 386 The necessity of endurance

content.ar:
نتیجه الطلب

وقال علیه السلام : من طلب شییا ناله او بعضه.
content.en:
The necessity of endurance

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: One who is in search of something will obtain it, at least some of it

title.en: Sayings 387 Recognition of good and evil

content.ar:
الخیر و الشر

وقال علیه السلام : ما خیر بخیر بعده النار، وما شر بشر بعده الجنه، وکل نعیم دون الجنه محقور کل بلا دون النار عافیه.
content.en:
Recognition of good and evil

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: That good is no good after which there is the Fire, and that hardship is no hardship after which there is Paradise. Every bliss other than Paradise is minor, and every calamity other than the Fire is comfort.

title.en: Sayings 388 Removing the poverty and health

content.ar:
الطریق الی معرفه السلامه

وقال علیه السلام : الا وان من البلا الفاقه واشد من الفاقه مرض البدن، واشد من مرض البدن مرض القلب الا وان من النعم سعه المال، وافضل من سعه المال صحه البدن، وافضل من صحه البدن تقوى القلب.
content.en:
Removing the poverty and health

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Beware that destitution is a calamity, but worse than destitution is ailment of the body, while worse than bodily ailment is the disease of the heart. Beware that plenty of wealth is a blessing, but better than plenty of wealth is the health of the body, while still better than the health of the body is the purity of the heart.

title.en: Sayings 389 The necessity for action

content.ar:
نصایح خالده

وقال علیه السلام : من ابطا به عمله لم یسرع به نسبه وفی روایه اخرى: من فاته حسب نفسه لم ینفعه حسب ابایه.
content.en:
The necessity for action

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: “Anyone whose his action keeps him behind his lineage cannot push him forward.” In another version, it is expressed thus: “Whoever misses his own personal attainments cannot gain any benefit from his forefathers’ attainments.”

title.en: Sayings 390 Correct planning for life

content.ar:
نظم المعیشه

وقال علیه السلام : للمومن ثلاث ساعات: فساعه یناجی فیها ربه، وساعه یرمه معاشه، وساعه یخلی بین نفسه وبین لذتها فیما یحل ویجمل ولیس للعاقل ان یکون شاخصا الا فی ثلاث: مرمه لمعاش، او خطوه فی معاد او لذه فی غیر محرم.
content.en:
Correct planning for life

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said The believer’s time has three periods: The period when he is in communion with Allah, the period when he manages for his livelihood, and the period when he is free to enjoy what is lawful and pleasant. It does not behoove a wise person to be away (from his house) save for three matters, namely: for the purposes of learning, or going for something for the next life, or for enjoying what is not prohibited.

title.en: Sayings 391 The necessity of leaving illegal act

content.ar:
مکاسب الزهد

وقال علیه السلام : ازهد فی الدنیا یبصرک الله عوراتها، ولا تغفل فلست بمغفول عنک!
content.en:
The necessity of leaving illegal act

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Abstain from the world so that Allah may show you its real evils, and do not be neglectful because (in any case) you will not be neglected.

title.en: Sayings 392 The way to know people

content.ar:
الطریق الی معرفه الرجال

وقال علیه السلام : تکلموا تعرفوا، فان المر مخبو تحت لسانه.
content.en:
The way to know people

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Speak so that you may be known since man is hidden under his tongue.

title.en: Sayings 393 How to deal with the world

content.ar:
اسلوب مواجهه الدنیا

وقال علیه السلام : خذ من الدنیا ما اتاک، وتول عما تولى عنک، فان انت لم تفعل فاجمل فی الطلب.
content.en:
How to deal with the world

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Take of the favors of the world whatever comes to you and keep away from what keeps away from you. If you cannot do so, be moderate in your pursuits.

title.en: Sayings 394 The worth of expression

content.ar:
فضل الکلام

وقال علیه السلام : رب قول انفذ من صول
content.en:
The worth of expression

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Many an expression is more effective than an onslaught.

title.en: Sayings 395 The value of contentment

content.ar:
فضل القناعه

وقال علیه السلام : کل مقتصرعلیه کاف.
content.en:
The value of contentment

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Every small thing that is contented upon suffices.

title.en: Sayings 396 A good way to live

content.ar:
نصایح خالده

وقال علیه السلام : المنیه ولا الدنیه والتقلل ولا التوسل ومن لم یعط قاعدا لم یعط قایما والدهر یومان: یوم لک، ویوم علیک؛ فاذا کان لک فلا تبطر، واذا کان علیک فاصبر!
content.en:
A good way to live

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Let it be death but not humiliation. Let it be little but not through others. Whoever does not get while sitting will not get by standing either. The world has two days: one for you and the other against you. When the day is for you, do not feel proud but when it is against you, endure it.

title.en: Sayings 397 Fine smells

content.ar:
افضل الطیب

وقال علیه السلام : نعم الطیب المسک، خفیف محمله، عطر ریحه.
content.en:
Fine smells

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The best scent is musk its weight is light while its smell is sweet.

title.en: Sayings 398 Leaving selfishness

content.ar:
الاجتناب من العجب

وقال علیه السلام : ضع فخرک، واحطط کبرک، واذکر قبرک.
content.en:
Leaving selfishness

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Put off boasting, give up self-conceit and remember your grave

title.en: Sayings 399 Interactive rights between father and the child
title.ar: الحکمة 399 الحقوق المتبادلة بین الوالد و الولد

content.ar:
الحقوق المتبادله بین الوالد و الولد

وقال علیه السلام : ان للولد على الوالد حقا، وان للوالد على الولد حقا فحق الوالد على الولد ان یطیعه فی کل شی الا فی معصیه الله سبحانه، وحق الولد على الوالد ان یحسن اسمه ویحسن ادبه ویعلمه القران.
content.en:
Interactive rights between father and the child

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The child has a right on the father while the father, too, has a right on the child. The right of the father on the child is that the latter should obey the former in every matter save in committing sins against Allah, the Glorified One, while the right of the child on the father is that the latter should give the first a beautiful name, provide him with good training and teach him the Quran.

title.en: Sayings 400 Knowledge of right and wrong

content.ar:
معرفه الحق و الباطل

وقال علیه السلام : العین حق، والرقى حق، والسحر حق، والفال حق، والطیره لیست بحق، والعدوى لیست بحق، والطیب نشره والعسل نشره، والرکوب نشره، والنظر الى الخضره نشره.
content.en:
Knowledge of right and wrong

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Evil effect of sight is right, charm is right, sorcery is right, and fa’l (auguring good) is right, while tayrah (auguring evil) is not right, and spreading of a disease from one to the other is not right. Scent gives pleasure, honey gives pleasure, riding gives pleasure and looking at greenery gives pleasure.

title.en: Sayings 401 Coordination with people

content.ar:
اسلوب مواجهه الناس

وقال علیه السلام : مقاربه الناس فی اخلاقهم امن من غوایلهم.
content.en:
Coordination with people

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Nearness with people in their manners brings about safety from their evil.

title.en: Sayings 402 The necessity of recognizing the position

content.ar:
معرفه الناس

وقال علیه السلام لبعض مخاطبیه، وقد تکلم بکلمه یستصغر مثله عن قول مثلها:

لقد طرت شکیرا،

هدرت سقبا

قال الرضى :

و الشکیر هاهنا: اول ما ینبت من ریش الطایر

قبل ان یقوى و یستحصف

والسقب الصغیر من الابل

و لا یهدر الا بعد ان یستفحل.
content.en:
The necessity of recognizing the position

Someone uttered an expression above his status. Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

You have started flying soon after growing feathers

and commenced grumbling before attaining youth.

Sayyid ar-Radi says the following:

Here, “shakir” [in the original Arabic text] means the first feathers that grow on a bird

before it is strong enough to fly.

And “saqb” means the young camel

who does not grumble unless it becomes mature.

title.en: Sayings 403 Avoiding from various job

content.ar:
الاجتناب من الاعمال المتنوعه

وقال علیه السلام : من اوما الى متفاوت خذلته الحیل.
content.en:
Avoiding from various job

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Whoever hankers after contraries gets no means of success.

title.en: Sayings 404 The meaning of “la hawla wala quwwata illa billah”
title.ar: الحکمة 404 تفسیر: لا حول و لا قوّة الّا باللّه

content.ar:
تفسیر: لا حول و لا قوه الا بالله

وقال علیه السلام وقد سیل عن معنى قولهم:

لا حول ولا قوه الا بالله

انا لا نملک مع الله شییا،

ولا نملک الا ما ملکنا،

فمتى ملکنا ما هو املک به منا

کلفنا،

ومتى اخذه منا

وضع تکلیفه عنا.
content.en:
The meaning of “la hawla wala quwwata illa billah”

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: On being asked about the meaning of the expression:

“la hawla wala quwwata illa billah” (there is no strength nor power except through Allah),

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: We are not masters of anything along with Allah,

and we are not masters of anything save what He makes us masters of.

So, when He makes us masters of anything of which He is a superior Lord over us,

He also assigns some duties to us.

And when He takes it away,

He takes away those duties as well.

title.en: Sayings 405 Ignorant pretenders

content.ar:
فضح المغیره بن شعبه

وقال علیه السلام لعمار بن یاسر رحمه الله،

وقد سمعه یراجع المغیره بن شعبه کلاما:

دعه یا عمار،

فانه لم یاخذ من الدین الا ما قاربته الدنیا،

وعلى عمد لبس على نفسه

لیجعل الشبهات عاذرا لسقطاته.
content.en:
Ignorant pretenders

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) heard ‘Ammar ibn Yasir (may Allah have mercy on him)

conversing with al-Mughirah ibn Shu‘bah and said:

Leave him alone, O ‘Ammar,

for he has entered religion only to the extent of his deriving advantage of the world,

and he has willfully involved himself in misgivings

in order to adopt them as cover for his shortcomings

title.en: Sayings 406 The riches and the poor's' behaviour

content.ar:
اخلاق الفقرا و الاغنیا

وقال علیه السلام : ما احسن تواضع الاغنیا للفقرا طلبا لما عند الله! واحسن منه تیه الفقرا على الاغنیا اتکالا على الله.
content.en:
The riches and the poor's' behaviour

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: It is good for the rich to show humility before the poor to seek rewards from Allah, but better than that is the pride of the poor towards the rich with trust in Allah.

title.en: Sayings 407 The value of wisdom

content.ar:
قیمه العقل

وقال علیه السلام : ما استودع الله امرا عقلا الا استنقذه به یوما ما!
content.en:
The value of wisdom

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Allah does not grant wisdom to a person except that some day He will save him from ruin with its help.

title.en: Sayings 408 The result of fighting for the truth

content.ar:
مصیر مکافحه الحق

وقال علیه السلام : من صارع الحق صرعه.
content.en:
The result of fighting for the truth

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Whoever clashes with the truth will be knocked down by it.

title.en: Sayings 409 The coordination between heart and eye
title.ar: الحکمة 409 ضرورة وحدة القلب و العین

content.ar:
ضروره وحده القلب و العین

وقال علیه السلام : القلب مصحف البصر
content.en:
The coordination between heart and eye

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The heart is the book of the eye

title.en: Sayings 410 The value of chastity

content.ar:
قیمه التقوی

وقال علیه السلام : التقى رییس الاخلاق.
content.en:
The value of chastity

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Fear of Allah is the chief trait of the human character.

title.en: Sayings 411 Respecting the master

content.ar:
فضل المعلم

وقال علیه السلام : لا تجعلن ذرب لسانک على من انطقک، وبلاغه قولک على من سددک.
content.en:
Respecting the master

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Do not try the sharpness of your tongue against the One Who gave you the power to speak, nor the eloquence of your speech against the One Who set you on the right path.

title.en: Sayings 412 The best way of self-regulating

content.ar:
طریقه تربیه النفس

وقال علیه السلام : کفاک ادبا لنفسک اجتناب ما تکرهه من غیرک.
content.en:
The best way of self-regulating

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: It is enough for your own discipline that you abstain from what you dislike from others.

title.en: Sayings 413 Enduring the disasters

content.ar:
الصبر و التغافل

وقال علیه السلام : من صبر صبر الاحرار، والا سلاسلو الاغمار.
content.en:
Enduring the disasters

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: One should endure like free people; otherwise, one should keep quite like the ignorant.

title.en: Sayings 414 enduring the disasters

content.ar:
الصبر و التغافل

وفی خبر اخر انه علیه السلام قال للاشعث بن قیس معزیا:

ان صبرت صبر الاکارم،

والا سلوت سلو البهایم
content.en:
enduring the disasters

In an incident, it is related that Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said to al-Ash‘ath ibn Qays by way of offering his condolences on the death of his son:

Either endure like great people

or else you will be impatient like animals.

title.en: Sayings 415 The reality of the world

content.ar:
حقیقه الدنیا

وقال علیه السلام فی صفه الدنیا:

تغر وتضر وتمر،

ان الله تعالى لم یرضها ثوابا لاولیایه،

ولا عقابا لاعدایه،

وان اهل الدنیا کرکب بینا هم حلوا

اذ صاح بهم سایقهم فارتحلوا
content.en:
The reality of the world

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

It deceives, it harms and it passes away.

Allah, the Sublime, did not approve it as a reward for His lovers

nor as a punishment for His enemies.

In fact, the people of this world are like those riders that as soon as they descended,

the driver called out to them and they marched off.

title.en: Sayings 416 How to deal with the world

content.ar:
اسلوب مواجهه الدنیا

وقال علیه السلام لابنه الحسن:

یا بنی لا تخلفن وراک شییا من الدنیا،

فانک تخلفه لاحد رجلین:

اما رجل عمل فیه بطاعه الله

فسعد بما شقیت به،

واما رجل عمل فیه بمعصیه الله

فشقی بما جمعت له

فکنت عونا له على معصیته،

ولیس احد هذین حقیقا ان توثره على نفسک.

قال الرضى :

و یروى هذا الکلام على وجه اخر، و هو:

اما بعد، فان الذی فی یدک من الدنیا

قد کان له اهل قبلک،

وهو صایر الى اهل بعدک،

وانما انت جامع لاحد رجلین:

رجل عمل فیما جمعته بطاعه الله

فسعد بما شقیت به،

او رجل عمل فیه بمعصیه الله

فشقی بما جمعت له،

ولیس احد هذین اهلا ان توثره على نفسک

تحمل له على ظهرک

فارج لمن مضى رحمه الله،

و لمن بقی رزق الله.
content.en:
How to deal with the world

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said to his son al-Hassan (PBUH):

O my son! Do not leave anything of this world behind you

because you will be leaving it for either of two sorts of persons:

a person who uses it in obeying Allah,

in this case he will acquire virtue through what was evil for you,

or a person who uses it in disobeying Allah and,

in that case, he will be reaping evil with what you collected for him.

So, you will be assisting him in his sinfulness.

Neither of these two deserves to be preferred by you over your own self.

Sayyid ar-Radi says the following:

“This saying is also related in another version thus:

Whatever of this world is now with you

was with others before you,

and it will pass to some others after you.

Thus, you are collecting things for either of two sorts of men:

a man who uses whatever you collected in obedience of Allah

and thus acquired virtues with what was evil for you,

or a man who uses it in disobeying Allah,

so you will be getting evil for what you collected.

Neither of these two is such that you should prefer him over your own self,

for you may burden yourself for his own sake.

Therefore, hope for the mercy of Allah for him who has passed away

and for Divine livelihood for him who survives.

title.en: Sayings 417 The conditions of asking forgiveness

content.ar:
شرایط الاستغفار

وقال علیه السلام لقایل قال بحضرته: استغفر الله.

ثکلتک امک،

اتدری ما الاستغفار؟

ان الاستغفار درجه العلیین،

وهو اسم واقع على سته معان:

اولها: الندم على ما مضى.

والثانی: العزم على ترک العود الیه ابدا

والثالث: ان تودی الى الـمخلوقین حقوقهم

حتى تلقى الله عزوجل املس لیس علیک تبعه

والرابع: ان تعمد الى کل فریضه علیک ضیعتها

فتودی حقها

والخامس: ان تعمد الى اللحم الذی نبت على السحت

فتذیبه بالاحزان،

حتى یلصق الجلد بالعظم،

وینشا بینهما لحم جدید

والسادس: ان تذیق الجسم الم الطاعه

کما اذقته حلاوه المعصیه

فعند ذلک تقول: استغفرالله.
content.en:
The conditions of asking forgiveness

Someone said “Astaghfirullah!” (I seek Allah’s forgiveness) before Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH). Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

May your mother may lose you!

Do you know what “istighfar” is?

“Istighfar” is meant for people of a high status.

It is a word that stands on six pillars:

The first is to repent over the past,

the second is to make a firm determination never to revert to it,

the third is to carry out all the rights of people

so that you may meet Allah quite clean with nothing to account for,

the fourth is to fulfil every obligation which you ignored (in the past)

so that you may now do justice with it,

the fifth is to aim at the flesh grown as a result of unlawful earning,

so that you may melt it by grief (of repentance)

till the skin touches the bone

and a new flesh grows between them,

and the sixth is to make the body taste the pain of obedience

as you (previously) made it taste the sweetness of disobedience.

It is only on such an occasion that you may say: “Astaghfirullah!”

title.en: Sayings 418 The result of endurance

content.ar:
فضل الحلم

وقال علیه السلام : الحلم عشیره
content.en:
The result of endurance

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Clemency is (like) kinsfolk.

title.en: Sayings 419 The problems of a human

content.ar:
معرفه الانسان

وقال علیه السلام : مسکین ابن ادم: مکتوم الاجل، مکنون العلل، محفوظ العمل، تولمه البقه، وتقتله الشرقه وتنتنه العرقه.
content.en:
The problems of a human

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: How wretched the son of Adam is! His death is hidden, his ailments are concealed his actions are preserved, the bite of a mosquito pains him, choking cause his death and sweat gives him a bad smell

title.en: Sayings 420 The way of dealing with lusts

content.ar:
طریق السلامه من الشهوات

وروی انه علیه السلام کان جالسا فی اصحابه،

فمرت بهم امراه جمیله،

فرمقها القوم بابصارهم.

فقال علیه السلام :

ان ابصار هذه الفحول طوامح

وان ذلک سبب هبابها

فاذا نظر احدکم الى امراه تعجبه

فلیلامس اهله،

فانما هی امراه کامراه.

فقال رجل من الخوارج: قاتله الله کافرا

ما افقهه.

فوثب القوم لیقتلوه

فقال علیه السلام : رویدا

انما هو سب بسب،

او عفو عن ذنب.
content.en:
The way of dealing with lusts

It is related that Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) was sitting with his companions

when a beautiful woman passed by

and they began to look at her.

It was then that Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

The eyes of these men are /covetous,

and such glances are the cause of their becoming covetous.

Whenever anyone of you sees a woman who attracts him,

he should meet his wife [for cohabitation]

because she is a woman just like his wife.

One of the Kharijites said: “May Allah kill this heretic!

How logical he is!

People then leapt towards him to kill him,

but Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: “Wait a little bit.

There should either be abuse [for an abuse]

or else pardoning from the offence.”

title.en: Sayings 421 The result of wisdom

content.ar:
مکاسب العقل

وقال علیه السلام : کفاک من عقلک ما اوضح لک سبیل غیک من رشدک
content.en:
The result of wisdom

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Suffices you if your wisdom distinguishes for you the path of going astray fromthe path of your guidance.

title.en: Sayings 422 The value of beneficence

content.ar:
فضل الاحسان

وقال علیه السلام : افعلوا الخیر ولا تحقروا منه شییا، فان صغیره کبیر وقلیله کثیر، ولا یقولن احدکم: ان احدا اولى بفعل الخیر منی فیکون والله کذلک، ان للخیر والشر اهلا، فمهما ترکتموه منهما کفاکموه اهله
content.en:
The value of beneficence

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Do good and do not regard any part of it small because its small is big and its little is much. None of you should say that “Another person is more deserving than me” in doing good. Otherwise, by Allah, it will really be so. There are people of good and evil. When you leave either of the two, others will perform it.

title.en: Sayings 423 The result of self-regulating

content.ar:
ثمرات تربیه النفس

وقال علیه السلام : من اصلح سریرته اصلح الله علانیته، ومن عمل لدینه کفاه الله امر دنیاه، ومن احسن فیما بینه وبین الله کفاه الله ما بینه وبین الناس.
content.en:
The result of self-regulating

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Whoever mends his inward self, Allah mends his outward self. Whoever performs acts for the sake of his religion, Allah accomplishes his acts of this world. Anyone whose dealings between himself and Allah are good, Allah turns the dealings between him and other people good, too.

title.en: Sayings 424 The value of wisdom and patience

content.ar:
نصایح خالده

وقال علیه السلام : الحلم غطا ساتر، والعقل حسام قاطع، فاستر خلل خلقک بحلمک، وقاتل هواک بعقلک
content.en:
The value of wisdom and patience

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Forbearance is a covering curtain, and wisdom is a sharp sword. Therefore, conceal the weaknesses in your conduct with forbearance and kill your desires with your wisdom

title.en: Sayings 425 Responsibility of the riches

content.ar:
مسوولیه الاغنیا

وقال علیه السلام : ان لله عبادا یختصهم بالنعم لمنافع العباد، فیقرها فی ایدیهم ما بذلوها، فاذا منعوها نزعها منهم، ثم حولها الى غیرهم.
content.en:
Responsibility of the riches

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: There are some creatures of Allah whom Allah particularly chooses for His favors so that they may be of benefit to other people. Therefore, He keeps such favors in their hands so long as they give them out to others. But when they deny them to others, He takes away the favors from them and gives them to others

title.en: Sayings 426 Two unstable things

content.ar:
منزله العافیه و الغنی

وقال علیه السلام : لا ینبغی للعبد ان یثق بخصلتین: العافیه، والغنى: بینا تراه معافى اذ سقم، وغنیا اذ افتقر.
content.en:
Two unstable things

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: It does not behoove a man to have trust in two things: health and riches, because there is many a man whom you see healthy but he soon falls and many a man whom you see rich but soon turns destitute.

title.en: Sayings 427 The situation of making complaint

content.ar:
اثار الشکوی

وقال علیه السلام : من شکا الحاجه الى مومن فکانما شکاها الى الله، ومن شکاها الى کافر فکانما شکاالله.
content.en:
The situation of making complaint

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Whoever complains about a need to a believer, it is as though he has complained about it to Allah. But whoever complains about it to an unbeliever, it is as though he complained about Allah.

title.en: Sayings 428 Real festival

content.ar:
حقیقه العید

وقال علیه السلام فی بعض الاعیاد: انما هو عید لمن قبل الله صیامه وشکر قیامه، وکل یوم لا یعصى الله فیه فهو یوم عید.
content.en:
Real festival

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said on the occasion of an ‘Id: It is an ‘Id for anyone whose fast is accepted by Allah and for whose prayers He is grateful, and (in fact) every day wherein no sin against Allah is committed is an ‘Id.

title.en: Sayings 429 The greatest regret

content.ar:
اعظم الحسرات

وقال علیه السلام : ان اعظم الحسرات یوم القیامه حسره رجل کسب مالا فی غیر طاعه الله، فورثه رجلا فانفقه فی طاعه الله سبحانه، فدخل به الجنه، ودخل الاول به النار.
content.en:
The greatest regret

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: On the Day of Judgement, the greatest regret will be felt by the man who earned wealth through sinful ways, although it is inherited by a person who spends it in obeying Allah, the Glorified One, and he will be awarded Paradise on that account while the first one will be dragged into the Fire on its account.

title.en: Sayings 430 The greatest loser

content.ar:
اخسر الناس

وقال علیه السلام : ان اخسر الناس صفقه واخیبهم سعیا، رجل اخلق بدنه فی طلب اماله، ولم تساعده المقادیر على ارادته، فخرج من الدنیا بحسرته، وقدم على الاخره بتبعته.
content.en:
The greatest loser

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The worst in bargaining and the most unsuccessful in striving is a man who exerts himself in seeking riches although fate does not help him in his aims and,consequently, he goes from this world in a sorrowful state while in the next world, too, he will face its ill consequences.

title.en: Sayings 431 Types of livelihood

content.ar:
انواع الرزق

وقال علیه السلام : الرزق رزقان: طالب، ومطلوب، فمن طلب الدنیا طلبه الموت حتى یخرجه عنها، من طلب الاخره طلبته الدنیا حتى یستوفی رزقه منها.
content.en:
Types of livelihood

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Livelihood is of two kinds: the seeker and the sought. Therefore, whoever hankers after this world, death traces him till it turns him out of it. But whoever hankers after the next world, worldly ease itself seeks him till he receives his livelihood from it

title.en: Sayings 432 The characteristics of Allah lovers

content.ar:
خصایص اولیاالله

وقال علیه السلام : ان اولیا الله هم الذین نظروا الى باطن الدنیا اذا نظر الناس الى ظاهرها، واشتغلوا باجلها اذا اشتغل الناس بعاجلها، فاماتوا منها ما خشوا ان یمیتهم وترکوا منها ما علموا انه سیترکهم، وراوا استکثار غیرهم منها استقلالا، ودرکهم لها فوتا، اعدا ما سالم الناس، وسلم ما عادى الناس! بهم علم الکتاب وبه علموا وبهم قام الکتاب وبه قاموا، لا یرون مرجوا فوق ما یرجون، ولا مخوفا فوق ما یخافون.
content.en:
The characteristics of Allah lovers

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The lovers of Allah are those who look at the inward side of the world, while the other people look at its outward side. They occupy themselves with its most remote benefits while the other people occupy themselves with the immediate benefits. They kill those things which they feared will have killed them, and they leave here in this world what they think will leave them. They took the amassing of wealth by others as a small matter and regarded it like losing. They are enemies of those things which others love while they love things which others hate. Through them, the Quran has been learned, and they have been given knowledge through the Quran. With them, the Quran is staying while they stand by the Quran. They do not see any objective of hope above what they hope and no objective of fear above what they fear.

title.en: Sayings 433 Considering mortality of pleasures

content.ar:
مال اللذات

وقال علیه السلام : اذکروا انقطاع اللذات، وبقا التبعات.
content.en:
Considering mortality of pleasures

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Remember that pleasures will pass away while the consequences will stay.

title.en: Sayings 434 Testing people

content.ar:
ضروره الاختبار

وقال علیه السلام :

اخبر تقله .

قال الرضى :

و من الناس من یروی هذا لرسول الله صلى الله علیه واله

و مما یقوی انه من کلام امیرالمومنین علیه السلام ،

ما حکاه ثعلب قال: حدثنا ابن الاعرابی قال: قال المامون :

لولا ان علیا علیه السلام ، قال: اخبر تقله ،

لقلت انا: اقله تخبر.
content.en:
Testing people

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said:

Try (a man) and you will hate him!

Sayyid ar-Radi says the following:

“Some people say that this saying was articulated by the Prophet (SAAW),

but what confirms that it is the saying of Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH)

is the statement related by Tha‘lab from Ibn al-Arabi, that is, that (caliph) al-Ma’mun said,

“If Ali had not said ‘ukhbur thiqlihi’ (try a man and you will hate him),”

I will have said: ‘aqlihi takhbur’ (hate a man in order to try him).”

title.en: Sayings 435 The great ethical values

content.ar:
نصایح خالده

وقال علیه السلام : ما کان الله لیفتح على عبد باب الشکر ویغلق عنه باب الزیاده، ولا لیفتح على عبد باب الدعا ویغلق عنه باب الاجابه، ولا لیفتح على عبد باب التوبه ویغلق عنه باب المغفره.
content.en:
The great ethical values

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: It is not that Allah, to Whom belongs Might and Majesty, may keep the door of gratitude open for a person and close the door of plenty upon him, or to open the door of prayer to a person and close the door of acceptance upon him, or to open the door of repentance on a person and close the door of forgiveness upon him

title.en: Sayings 436 Recognizing the honourable persons

content.ar:
الاولی بالکرم

وقال علیه السلام : اولى الناس بالکرم من عرفت به الکرام.
content.en:
Recognizing the honourable persons

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: The most appropriate person for an honorable status is who the people of honor are known due to him.

title.en: Sayings 437 Justice  and  generosity

content.ar:
العدل و الجود

وسیل علیه السلام ایما افضل: العدل، او الجود؟

فقال: العدل یضع الامور مواضعها،

والجود یخرجها عن جهتها،

والعدل سایس عام،

والجود عارض خاص،

فالعدل اشرفهما وافضلهما.
content.en:
Justice and generosity

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) was asked: “Which of the two is better: justice or generosity?”

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) replied: “Justice puts things in their places

while generosity takes them out from their directions.

Justice is the general caretaker,

while generosity is a particular benefit.

Consequently, justice is superior and more distinguished of the two.”

title.en: Sayings 438 Ignorance and hostility

content.ar:
الجهل و و العداوه

وقال علیه السلام : الناس اعدا ما جهلوا.
content.en:
Ignorance and hostility

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: People are enemies of what they do not know.

title.en: Sayings 439 True asceticism

content.ar:
حقیقه الزهد

وقال علیه السلام : الزهد کله بین کلمتین من القران: قال الله عزوجل: لکیلا تاسوا على ما فاتکم ، و لا تفرحوا بما اتاکم فمن لم یاس على الماضی، ولم یفرح فقد اخذ الزهد بطرفیه.
content.en:
True asceticism

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said The whole of asceticism is confined between two expressions of the Quran: Allah, the Glorified One, says: “... lest you should distress yourselves because of what you miss and be over-joyous for what He has granted you” (Quran, 57: 23). Whoever does not grieve over what he misses and does not revel over what comes to him acquires asceticism from both its sides.

title.en: Sayings 440 The role of dreaming in life

content.ar:
النوم و فسخ العزایم

وقال علیه السلام : ما انقض النوم لعزایم الیوم
content.en:
The role of dreaming in life

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: What a breaker is sleep for the resolutions of the day!

title.en: Sayings 441 Job experience and knowing the directors

content.ar:
کیف یعرف الرجال

وقال علیه السلام : الولایات مضامیر الرجال .
content.en:
Job experience and knowing the directors

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Governing power is the proving ground for people

title.en: Sayings 442 The best town

content.ar:
خیر البلاد

وقال علیه السلام : لیس بلد باحق بک من بلد، خیر البلاد ما حملک
content.en:
The best town

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: No town has a greater right on you than the other. The best town for you is that which bears you.

title.en: Sayings 443 Characteristics of Malek Ashtar

content.ar:
خصایص مالک بن الاشتر

وقال علیه السلام : وقد جاه نعی الاشتر رحمه الله:

مالک وما مالک!

والله لو کان جبلا

لکان فندا،

ولو کان حجرا لکان صلدا،

لا یرتقیه الحافر،

ولا یوفی علیهالطایر.

قال الرضى :

الفند: المنفرد من الجبال.
content.en:
Characteristics of Malek Ashtar

When the news of the death of (Malik) al-Ashtar (may Allah have mercy on him), reached Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH), he said:

Malik, what a man Malik was!

By Allah! If he had been a mountain,

he would have been a big one (a find),

and if he had been a stone, he would have been quite stiff.

No horseman could have reached it

and no bird could have flown over it.

Sayyid ar-Radi says the following:

“find” means a lonely mountain (rising in height above the others in the range).

title.en: Sayings 444 The value of continuing a task

content.ar:
فضل استمرار فی العمل

وقال علیه السلام : قلیل مدوم علیه خیر من کثیر مملول منه. .
content.en:
The value of continuing a task

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: A little that lasts is better than much that brings grief.

title.en: Sayings 445 Anthropology

content.ar:
معرفه الانسان

وقال علیه السلام : اذا کان فی رجل خله رایعه فانتظر اخواتها.
content.en:
Anthropology

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said If a man possesses a revealing quality, wait and see his other qualities

title.en: Sayings 446 The necessity to pay debts

content.ar:
وجوب ادا الدیون

وقال علیه السلام لغالب بن صعصعه ابی الفرزدق، فی کلام دار بینهما:

ما فعلت ابلک الکثیره؟

قال: ذعذعتها الحقوق یا امیرالمومنین

فقال علیه السلام : ذاک احمد سبلها.
content.en:
The necessity to pay debts

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) asked Ghalib ibn Sa‘sa‘ah, father of al-Farazdaq (the famous Arab poet), during a conversation between them:

“What about the large number of your camels?!”

The man replied: “They have been swept away by (the performing of) obligations, O Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH)!

” Whereupon Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: “That is the most praiseworthy way of (losing) them.”

title.en: Sayings 447 The necessity of knowing the rules of religious laws about business

content.ar:
ضروره التفقه فی التجاره

وقال علیه السلام : من اتجر بغیر فقه ارتطم فی الربا.
content.en:
The necessity of knowing the rules of religious laws about business

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Whoever trades without knowing the rules of religious law will be involved in usury.

title.en: Sayings 448 The way to deal with the distresses

content.ar:
اسلوب مواجهه المصایب

وقال علیه السلام : من عظم صغار المصایب ابتلاه الله بکبارها.
content.en:
The way to deal with the distresses

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Whoever regards small distresses as big, Allah involves him in big ones.

title.en: Sayings 449 The way to confront the sensualism

content.ar:
اسلوب مواجهه الشهوات

وقال علیه السلام : من کرمت علیه نفسه هانت علیه شهوته.
content.en:
The way to confront the sensualism

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Whoever maintains his own respect in view, his desires appear light to him.

title.en: Sayings 450 Avoidance from kidding

content.ar:
الاجتناب من المزاح

وقال علیه السلام : ما مزح امرو مزحه الا مج من عقله مجه. .
content.en:
Avoidance from kidding

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Whenever a man cuts a joke, he separates himself away a bit from his wit.

title.en: Sayings 451 How to deal with the people

content.ar:
اسلوب مواجهه الناس

وقال علیه السلام : زهدک فی راغب فیک نقصان حظ، ورغبتک فی زاهد فیک ذل نفس.
content.en:
How to deal with the people

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Your turning away from the one who inclines towards you is a loss of your share of advantage while your inclining towards one who turns away from you is self- humiliation

title.en: Sayings 452 Recognition of the riches and poor

content.ar:
الفقر و الغنی الحقیقیان

وقال علیه السلام : الغنى والفقر بعد العرض على الله.
content.en:
Recognition of the riches and poor

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Riches and destitution will follow presentation before Allah.

title.en: Sayings 453 The reason for deviation of  Al-Zubayr

content.ar:
فضح الزبیر بن العوام

وقال علیه السلام : ما زال الزبیر رجلا منا اهل البیت حتى نشا ابنه المشووم عبدالله.
content.en:
The reason for deviation of Al-Zubayr

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: Al-Zubayr remained a man from our house till his wretched son ‘Abdullah came forth.

title.en: Sayings 454 How to remove the selfishness

content.ar:
طریقه اقتلاع العجب

وقال علیه السلام : ما لابن ادم والفخر: اوله نطفه، واخره جیفه، و لا یرزق نفسه، ولا یدفع حتفه.
content.en:
How to remove the selfishness

Imam Ali ibn Abu Talib (PBUH) said: What has a man to do with vanity? His origin is semen, his end is a carcass, while he cannot feed himself nor ward off death?

title.en: Sayings 455 The greatest poet

content.ar:
اشعر الشعرا

وسیل علیه السلام : من اشعر الشعرا؟

فقال: ان القوم لم یجروا فی حلبه تعرف الغایه عند قصبتها

فان کان ولا بد فالملک الضلیل

قال الرضى :

یرید امرا القیس
content.en:
The greatest poet
